questions.prompt: Which AWS service or principle most directly enables a company to convert capital expense (CapEx) into operational expense (OpEx)?
questions.explanation: A core advantage of cloud computing is trading up-front hardware spend (CapEx) for variable, on-demand spend (OpEx). Pay-as-you-go pricing is the mechanism that makes this possible — you only pay for what you use, when you use it.

questions.prompt: A startup wants to deploy a new application in minutes instead of weeks. Which benefit of the AWS Cloud most directly addresses this need?

options.text: Trading fixed expense for variable expense

questions.explanation: Agility — the ability to rapidly provision, experiment, and deploy resources — is the AWS benefit that turns weeks-long provisioning into minutes-long provisioning.

questions.prompt: Which two statements describe the AWS Well-Architected Framework? (Choose two.)

options.text: It is a set of pre-built CloudFormation templates customers must use.
It provides a consistent approach for evaluating architectures against best practices.
It is organized around six pillars including security, reliability, and cost optimization.
It guarantees compliance with all industry regulations.
It is only relevant to net-new applications, not existing workloads.

questions.explanation: The Well-Architected Framework is a guidance set for evaluating workloads against AWS best practices. It is organized around six pillars: Operational Excellence, Security, Reliability, Performance Efficiency, Cost Optimization, and Sustainability. It is not a template, does not guarantee compliance, and applies to both new and existing workloads.

questions.prompt: Which AWS global infrastructure component is a logical grouping of one or more discrete data centers within a single AWS Region?
questions.explanation: An Availability Zone (AZ) is one or more discrete data centers with redundant power, networking, and connectivity in an AWS Region. Edge locations serve CloudFront; Local Zones extend an AZ closer to end users; a Region contains multiple AZs.

questions.prompt: Which AWS Cloud benefit best matches the statement: "AWS can deploy a workload to multiple Regions worldwide in minutes"?
questions.explanation: "Going global in minutes" is one of the six advantages of cloud computing and refers specifically to the ability to deploy a workload to multiple Regions around the world quickly.

questions.prompt: Which cloud deployment model describes an organization running some workloads in AWS and other workloads on-premises, connected by a private network?
questions.explanation: A hybrid deployment connects cloud infrastructure (AWS) to existing on-premises infrastructure. Multi-cloud refers to using multiple cloud providers; private cloud is fully on-premises; cloud-native is purely cloud-based.

questions.prompt: Which AWS Well-Architected pillar focuses on the ability of a workload to recover from infrastructure or service disruptions?
questions.explanation: The Reliability pillar addresses the ability of a workload to perform its intended function correctly and consistently — including recovering from disruptions and dynamically acquiring resources to meet demand.

questions.prompt: Which design principle is recommended by the AWS Well-Architected Framework for building reliable systems?

options.text: Manually provision servers to reduce automation errors
Scale vertically by increasing instance size whenever possible
Avoid testing recovery procedures in production-like environments

questions.explanation: Automatically recovering from failure (e.g., self-healing systems that detect and replace unhealthy instances) is a core reliability design principle. Manual provisioning, exclusive vertical scaling, and skipping recovery testing are explicit anti-patterns.

questions.prompt: Which AWS infrastructure component is used by Amazon CloudFront to cache content close to end users?
questions.explanation: CloudFront uses a global network of edge locations (and regional edge caches) to deliver cached content close to viewers, reducing latency. Regions and AZs host origin services; Local Zones extend Regions for low-latency compute.

questions.prompt: Which characteristic best describes elasticity in the AWS Cloud?

options.text: Servers are provisioned for peak load and remain at that size at all times
Resources can be scaled up or down automatically in response to demand
Workloads can run on dedicated physical hardware
Customers receive volume discounts on storage usage

questions.explanation: Elasticity is the ability to acquire resources as you need them and release them when you don't, often automatically. Provisioning for peak (option A) is the opposite of elasticity.

questions.prompt: Which statement best describes the AWS shared responsibility model?

options.text: AWS is responsible for all aspects of customer applications and data
Customers are responsible for the physical security of AWS data centers
AWS is responsible for security "of" the cloud; customers are responsible for security "in" the cloud
The customer is solely responsible for the durability of S3 objects

questions.explanation: AWS owns security of the cloud (physical facilities, hardware, hypervisor, managed-service infrastructure). Customers own security in the cloud (their data, OS configuration on EC2, IAM users and policies, application code).

questions.prompt: A company wants to design a workload that can survive the loss of an entire data center without downtime. Which approach should it use?

options.text: Deploy the workload to a single Availability Zone with Auto Scaling
Deploy the workload to multiple Availability Zones in the same Region
Deploy the workload to a single edge location
Deploy the workload to AWS Local Zones only

questions.explanation: Each AZ is a logically isolated set of data centers. Distributing instances across multiple AZs in a Region means the loss of one AZ does not take the workload down. Single-AZ deployments cannot survive a full data-center loss.

questions.prompt: Which Well-Architected pillar covers the ability to use computing resources efficiently and to maintain that efficiency as demand changes?
questions.explanation: Performance Efficiency is about selecting the right resource types and sizes for the workload, and continuing to do so as requirements evolve. Cost Optimization focuses on price; Operational Excellence on running and monitoring.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an example of a managed service?

options.text: Amazon EC2 instance running a self-installed MySQL server
An on-premises database on owned hardware
A custom database running on AWS Outposts hardware managed by the customer

questions.explanation: Amazon RDS is a managed database service: AWS handles patching, backups, and provisioning of the underlying engine. Running MySQL on an EC2 instance is self-managed even though the EC2 service itself is managed at the hypervisor level.

questions.prompt: Which AWS benefit is illustrated by the ability of multiple customers to share underlying physical hardware while remaining logically isolated?
questions.explanation: Multitenancy lets AWS aggregate demand from many customers across shared infrastructure, which produces economies of scale — passed back to customers as lower prices.

questions.prompt: Which design principle aligns with the Sustainability pillar of the Well-Architected Framework?

options.text: Over-provisioning resources to ensure peak performance
Maximizing utilization of provisioned resources to reduce environmental impact
Storing all logs indefinitely to ensure auditability
Running workloads only in on-premises data centers

questions.explanation: Sustainability emphasizes maximizing utilization (right-sizing, reducing idle resources, using managed services with shared infrastructure) and adopting more efficient hardware/software where possible.

questions.prompt: Under the AWS shared responsibility model, which of the following is the customer's responsibility for an Amazon EC2 instance?

options.text: Replacing failed disks in the data center
Securing physical access to AWS facilities

questions.explanation: For EC2 (IaaS), the customer is responsible for the guest OS — including patches, security configuration, and applications. AWS is responsible for the hypervisor, host hardware, and physical facilities.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service helps protect web applications against common exploits such as SQL injection and cross-site scripting?
questions.explanation: AWS WAF (Web Application Firewall) lets customers define rules that filter HTTP/S traffic, blocking common exploits at the application layer. Shield protects against DDoS; Inspector scans for software vulnerabilities; Config tracks resource configuration.

questions.prompt: Which IAM best practice should be applied to the AWS account root user?

options.text: Use the root user for all daily administrative tasks
Create programmatic access keys for the root user and embed them in applications
Enable multi-factor authentication (MFA) on the root user and avoid using it for daily tasks
Share the root user credentials across the administrative team

questions.explanation: The root user has unrestricted access. Best practice: enable MFA, create IAM users (or use IAM Identity Center) for day-to-day tasks, and lock away the root credentials.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service provides DDoS protection at no additional cost for all AWS customers?
questions.explanation: AWS Shield Standard is enabled by default for all AWS customers at no extra cost and protects against the most common network and transport-layer DDoS attacks. Shield Advanced is a paid tier with deeper protection and 24/7 DDoS response team access.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service continuously analyzes account activity and uses machine learning to detect threats such as compromised credentials or cryptocurrency mining?
questions.explanation: Amazon GuardDuty is a threat-detection service that continuously monitors CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs, and DNS logs using ML and threat intelligence to surface findings like unusual API calls or coin-mining traffic.

questions.prompt: A company needs to download AWS compliance reports such as SOC 1, SOC 2, and ISO 27001 for its auditors. Which service should it use?
questions.explanation: AWS Artifact is the central on-demand portal for AWS compliance reports (SOC, ISO, PCI, FedRAMP, etc.) and select online agreements like Business Associate Addendums.

questions.prompt: Which IAM entity is used to grant permissions to AWS services (e.g., letting an EC2 instance call other AWS APIs)?
questions.explanation: IAM roles deliver temporary credentials and are the recommended way to grant AWS services (such as an EC2 instance via an instance profile) the permissions they need — no long-lived keys on the instance.

questions.prompt: Which two practices align with the AWS principle of least privilege? (Choose two.)

options.text: Grant administrator access to all developers for convenience
Use specific IAM policies that grant only the permissions required for the task
Regularly review and remove unused IAM permissions
Embed long-lived access keys in application source code
Share IAM user credentials between team members

questions.explanation: Least privilege means starting from minimum permissions and granting only what is needed, then auditing and reducing further over time. Blanket admin access, embedded keys, and shared credentials all violate this principle.

questions.prompt: Which service uses machine learning to automatically discover and classify sensitive data such as PII stored in Amazon S3?
questions.explanation: Amazon Macie uses ML to identify and protect sensitive data (PII, PHI, financial data) in S3 buckets and report on exposure or policy violations.

questions.prompt: Which service records API calls made in your AWS account and is essential for security auditing?
questions.explanation: CloudTrail records account activity (who called what API, when, from where). CloudWatch handles metrics and logs; Config tracks resource configuration history; X-Ray traces application requests.

questions.prompt: A customer needs to store and rotate database credentials, API keys, and other secrets. Which service should they use?

options.text: AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store with SecureString and no rotation

questions.explanation: AWS Secrets Manager is purpose-built for storing secrets and has built-in automatic rotation (notably for RDS). Parameter Store can store secrets but doesn't natively rotate them.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service helps centrally manage encryption keys and uses hardware security modules (HSMs) validated under FIPS 140-2?
questions.explanation: AWS KMS (Key Management Service) is the managed service for creating, controlling, and auditing encryption keys. KMS uses FIPS 140-2 validated HSMs to protect keys.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service should be used to scan EC2 instances and container images for software vulnerabilities and unintended network exposure?
questions.explanation: Amazon Inspector performs automated, continuous vulnerability assessment of EC2 instances and ECR container images, and surfaces network reachability issues.

questions.prompt: Which feature provides an additional layer of authentication beyond a username and password for AWS users?
questions.explanation: MFA combines something you know (password) with something you have (a hardware key, virtual MFA device, or biometric). AWS strongly recommends MFA on the root user and all privileged IAM identities.

questions.prompt: A company wants to track configuration changes to AWS resources and assess them against desired configurations. Which service fits?
questions.explanation: AWS Config continuously records resource configurations and evaluates them against rules (e.g., "S3 buckets must not be public"), surfacing drift over time. CloudTrail records the API calls that caused the change.

questions.prompt: Which statement about Amazon S3 server-side encryption is correct?

options.text: Server-side encryption is not available for S3
S3 supports server-side encryption with S3-managed keys (SSE-S3), KMS keys (SSE-KMS), and customer-provided keys (SSE-C)
Server-side encryption is only available on S3 Glacier classes
Server-side encryption requires the customer to run an EC2 instance to encrypt objects

questions.explanation: S3 offers three server-side encryption options: SSE-S3 (S3-managed keys), SSE-KMS (AWS KMS keys), and SSE-C (customer-provided keys). All are managed at the bucket/object level and require no additional infrastructure.

questions.prompt: Which AWS resource is used to control inbound and outbound traffic at the instance level inside a VPC?
questions.explanation: A security group is a stateful, instance-level firewall. Network ACLs are stateless and operate at the subnet level. Route tables direct traffic; an internet gateway provides VPC internet egress.

questions.prompt: Which two of the following are AWS responsibilities under the shared responsibility model? (Choose two.)

options.text: Patching customer EC2 guest operating systems
Maintaining hardware that hosts AWS services
Configuring customer IAM users and policies
Encrypting customer data in transit over the application layer

questions.explanation: AWS owns physical infrastructure: data center security, hardware, networking, hypervisor. Customers own their guest OS patching, IAM configuration, and application-level encryption decisions.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service offers a managed directory service that supports Active Directory-aware applications?
questions.explanation: AWS Directory Service provides managed directories, including AWS Managed Microsoft AD for workloads that need Active Directory features. Cognito handles app user pools; IAM Identity Center provides workforce SSO to AWS accounts and apps.

questions.prompt: Which option is the most appropriate way to grant a third-party auditor temporary, time-limited access to read AWS resources?

options.text: Create a long-lived IAM user with administrator access and share the credentials
Create an IAM role with the necessary read-only permissions and let the auditor assume it
Make all relevant S3 buckets public for the duration of the audit

questions.explanation: Cross-account IAM roles deliver temporary credentials scoped to specific permissions and time. Long-lived users, the root account, or public buckets all violate least privilege.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service provides resizable virtual servers in the cloud?
questions.explanation: Amazon EC2 (Elastic Compute Cloud) is the AWS service for provisioning virtual machines. S3 is object storage, Lambda is serverless functions, EFS is shared file storage.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service lets you run code without provisioning or managing servers, paying only for the compute time you consume?
questions.explanation: AWS Lambda is the serverless compute service: you upload code, AWS runs it in response to events, and you pay per request and execution duration.

questions.prompt: Which AWS storage class is designed for long-term archival with retrieval times of minutes to hours, at the lowest cost?
questions.explanation: S3 Glacier Deep Archive is the lowest-cost S3 storage class, designed for data accessed once or twice a year with retrieval times of around 12 hours (or 48 hours for bulk).

questions.prompt: Which AWS database service is a managed NoSQL key-value store offering single-digit millisecond performance at any scale?
questions.explanation: Amazon DynamoDB is a managed NoSQL (key-value and document) database designed for single-digit millisecond latency. RDS and Aurora are relational; Redshift is a data warehouse.

questions.prompt: Which service is a fully managed, petabyte-scale data warehouse optimized for analytics?
questions.explanation: Amazon Redshift is the managed data warehouse purpose-built for large-scale analytics queries. Aurora and RDS are transactional databases; DocumentDB is a MongoDB-compatible document store.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service distributes incoming application traffic across multiple targets such as EC2 instances?
questions.explanation: Elastic Load Balancing (ALB, NLB, GLB) distributes incoming traffic across targets. Route 53 is DNS; Auto Scaling adjusts capacity; Direct Connect provides dedicated network connectivity to AWS.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service provides a content delivery network (CDN) to serve content from edge locations close to users?
questions.explanation: Amazon CloudFront is the AWS CDN, caching content at hundreds of edge locations. Global Accelerator improves routing to regional endpoints but is not a CDN; Route 53 is DNS; Direct Connect is private connectivity.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service automatically adjusts the number of EC2 instances based on demand?
questions.explanation: Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling adds and removes instances to match demand. ELB distributes traffic but doesn't change capacity. CloudWatch provides the metrics that drive Auto Scaling decisions.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service provides a managed, scalable DNS service?
questions.explanation: Amazon Route 53 is AWS's authoritative DNS service. It also offers domain registration and health-check-based routing policies.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service allows on-premises data centers to be extended into AWS over a dedicated, private network connection?
questions.explanation: AWS Direct Connect provides a dedicated, private network link between on-premises and AWS — higher bandwidth and lower jitter than an internet VPN. VPN traverses the public internet; VPC peering connects VPCs.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service is a managed, MySQL- and PostgreSQL-compatible relational database that delivers up to several times the throughput of standard open-source engines?
questions.explanation: Amazon Aurora is a MySQL- and PostgreSQL-compatible managed relational database with cloud-optimized storage that delivers significantly higher throughput than the community engines.

questions.prompt: Which AWS storage service offers a shared file system that can be mounted by many EC2 instances simultaneously?
questions.explanation: Amazon EFS (Elastic File System) is a managed NFS file system that multiple EC2 instances can mount concurrently. EBS volumes attach to a single instance (with exceptions); S3 is object storage.

questions.prompt: Which service is used to provision AWS infrastructure as code using declarative templates?
questions.explanation: AWS CloudFormation lets you describe AWS resources in YAML or JSON templates and provisions them as managed "stacks." CodeDeploy deploys application code; OpsWorks is configuration management; EC2 Image Builder produces AMIs.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service is a managed message queuing service that decouples application components?
questions.explanation: Amazon SQS (Simple Queue Service) is a managed message queue. SNS is pub/sub topics; EventBridge is an event bus; Kinesis is real-time streaming.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service is a managed pub/sub messaging service that delivers messages to multiple subscribers including email, SMS, Lambda, and SQS?
questions.explanation: Amazon SNS is the managed pub/sub service for fanout to many subscriber types. SQS is point-to-point queuing; MQ is a managed traditional message broker; Step Functions orchestrates workflows.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service collects metrics, logs, and alarms for AWS resources and applications?
questions.explanation: Amazon CloudWatch is the observability service for metrics, logs, dashboards, and alarms. CloudTrail records API activity; Config tracks configuration; X-Ray traces application requests.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service should be used to migrate terabytes-to-petabytes of data into AWS when network transfer would take too long?
questions.explanation: The AWS Snow Family (Snowcone, Snowball Edge, formerly Snowmobile) provides physical devices to move very large datasets when network bandwidth is impractical. DataSync is online transfer over the network.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service makes it easy to migrate on-premises file or block data to AWS while keeping low-latency local access?
questions.explanation: AWS Storage Gateway provides hybrid cloud storage: an on-premises appliance with cached local access backed by S3 / EBS / Glacier. Outposts is a fully on-prem AWS rack; FSx is managed file systems in AWS.

questions.prompt: Which two AWS compute services run containers without requiring the customer to manage the underlying EC2 instances? (Choose two.)

options.text: Amazon ECS on EC2 launch type
Amazon EC2 with Docker installed manually

questions.explanation: AWS Fargate is the serverless compute engine for containers — used with both ECS and EKS — so the customer doesn't provision EC2 instances. The EC2 launch type and self-managed node groups require managing the underlying instances.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service is a fully managed Kubernetes service?
questions.explanation: Amazon EKS (Elastic Kubernetes Service) is the managed upstream-compatible Kubernetes offering. ECS is the AWS-native container orchestrator; Lambda is serverless functions; Batch is for batch jobs.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service provides a logically isolated network where customers can launch AWS resources?
questions.explanation: Amazon VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) is the AWS networking service for isolated virtual networks. Route 53 is DNS; Direct Connect is private connectivity to AWS; Transit Gateway connects multiple VPCs.

questions.prompt: Which AWS Support plan provides access to a designated Technical Account Manager (TAM) and is recommended for mission-critical workloads?
questions.explanation: Enterprise Support (and Enterprise On-Ramp, with shared pooled TAM access) includes a designated Technical Account Manager. Business does not include a TAM; Developer and Basic include neither.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service provides recommendations to optimize cost, performance, security, fault tolerance, and service limits?
questions.explanation: AWS Trusted Advisor offers five categories of best-practice checks: cost optimization, performance, security, fault tolerance, and service limits. Cost Explorer visualizes spend; Budgets sets alerts; Compute Optimizer focuses on right-sizing compute.

questions.prompt: Which EC2 purchase option offers the largest discount in exchange for a 1- or 3-year commitment to a consistent amount of compute usage (in $/hour)?
questions.explanation: Savings Plans commit to a dollar-per-hour spend for 1 or 3 years in exchange for steep discounts (Compute Savings Plans being the most flexible). Spot offers larger discounts but can be interrupted; On-Demand has no commitment but is full price.

questions.prompt: Which AWS purchasing option is best suited for fault-tolerant workloads that can be interrupted, in exchange for up to 90% discount?
questions.explanation: Spot Instances let you bid on unused EC2 capacity at discounts up to about 90%, but AWS can reclaim them with two minutes' notice. Ideal for stateless, fault-tolerant, or batch workloads.

questions.prompt: Which AWS feature consolidates billing across multiple AWS accounts and can produce volume discounts that single accounts could not earn alone?
questions.explanation: Consolidated billing in AWS Organizations rolls multiple member accounts into one payer account, aggregating usage so tiered-pricing services (like S3) reach volume discount tiers sooner.

questions.prompt: Which two services are always free under the AWS Free Tier? (Choose two.)

options.text: Amazon S3 (5 GB free for 12 months)
Amazon EC2 t2.micro (750 hours/month for 12 months)
AWS Lambda (1 million requests per month, always free)

questions.explanation: AWS IAM is an "always free" service — there is no charge for IAM itself. Lambda has an "always free" tier of 1M requests and 400,000 GB-seconds per month. The S3, EC2, and RDS allowances are 12-month introductory free tier, not always-free.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service lets a customer set custom cost or usage thresholds and receive alerts when they are approached or exceeded?
questions.explanation: AWS Budgets lets customers set custom thresholds for cost, usage, RI/SP coverage, etc., and triggers email or SNS alerts. Cost Explorer visualizes historical spend; the Pricing Calculator estimates future spend.

questions.prompt: Which AWS Support plan is the lowest paid tier and provides 24x7 access to Cloud Support engineers via email, chat, and phone for production-system-impaired and higher severities?
questions.explanation: Business Support adds 24x7 access to Cloud Support engineers (phone/chat/email) and full Trusted Advisor checks. Developer is business-hours email-only; Basic has no engineer support; Enterprise On-Ramp adds TAM pool access.

questions.prompt: Which of the following are benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud? (Choose two.)
questions.explanation: Migrating to AWS delivers operational resilience (multi-AZ / multi-Region designs) and business agility (rapid provisioning and experimentation). Amazon.com retail discounts, marketing terms like "business excellence," and staff retention are not AWS Cloud value-framework benefits.

questions.prompt: A company is planning to replace its physical on-premises compute servers with AWS serverless compute services. The company wants to be able to take advantage of advanced technologies quickly after the migration. Which pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework does this plan represent?
questions.explanation: The Performance Efficiency pillar's design principles include "democratize advanced technologies" and "go global in minutes" — exactly the goal of adopting serverless to use new capabilities quickly.

questions.prompt: A company is planning to run a global marketing application in the AWS Cloud. The application will feature videos that can be viewed by users. The company must ensure that all users can view these videos with low latency. Which AWS service should the company use to meet this requirement?
questions.explanation: Amazon CloudFront is the AWS CDN — it caches video content at edge locations worldwide so global users get low-latency playback. Auto Scaling adjusts compute, ELB distributes regional traffic, Kinesis Video Streams ingests live video.

questions.prompt: Which component of the AWS global infrastructure is made up of one or more discrete redundant power, networking, and connectivity?
questions.explanation: An Availability Zone is one or more discrete data centers with redundant power, networking, and connectivity in an AWS Region. A Region is a geographic area of AZs; edge locations cache CloudFront content; Outposts is on-prem AWS hardware.

questions.prompt: Which pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework refers to the ability of a system to recover from infrastructure or service disruptions and dynamically acquire computing resources to meet demand?
questions.explanation: Reliability is the pillar that addresses recovering from disruptions and dynamically acquiring resources to meet demand — both stated outcomes in the prompt.

questions.prompt: Who is responsible for configuration management under the AWS shared responsibility model?

options.text: It is solely the responsibility of the customer.
It is solely the responsibility of AWS.
It is shared between AWS and the customer.
It is not part of the AWS shared responsibility model.

questions.explanation: Configuration management is explicitly a shared control: AWS configures the infrastructure devices it manages, customers configure their own guest OSes, databases, and applications.

questions.prompt: Which duties are the responsibility of a company that is using AWS Lambda? (Choose two.)
questions.explanation: With Lambda, AWS handles the OS, CPU/memory provisioning, and infrastructure security. The customer's responsibilities are writing/updating the function code and securing what is inside that code (input validation, secrets handling, IAM scope).

questions.prompt: Which compute hosting model should be accounted for in the Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) when undertaking a cost analysis that allows physical isolation of a customer workload?
questions.explanation: Dedicated Hosts give a customer an entire physical server, providing physical isolation from other AWS customers. Reserved Instances, On-Demand, and No-Upfront RIs are pricing/commitment models, not isolation models.

questions.prompt: Who is accountable for security and compliance under the AWS shared responsibility model?

options.text: AWS and the customer share responsibility.
AWS shares responsibility with the relevant governing body.

questions.explanation: Security and compliance are a shared responsibility: AWS owns security "of" the cloud and the customer owns security "in" the cloud.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a critical design concept for architecting cloud applications?
questions.explanation: Elasticity — acquiring resources when you need them and releasing them when you don't — is a foundational cloud design principle. Provisioning for peak or always picking the largest instance is the opposite of cloud-native design.

questions.prompt: Which AWS services or features provide disaster recovery solutions for Amazon EC2 instances? (Choose two.)

options.text: Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) snapshots

questions.explanation: EC2 AMIs let you re-launch identical instances in another AZ or Region; EBS snapshots back up volumes and can be copied across Regions. RIs are pricing, Shield is DDoS protection, GuardDuty is threat detection — none are DR mechanisms.

questions.prompt: A company wants to review its monthly costs of using Amazon EC2 and Amazon RDS for the past year. Which AWS service or tool provides this information?
questions.explanation: AWS Cost Explorer visualizes historical spend by service over rolling time windows up to 12 months. Trusted Advisor flags optimization opportunities, Forecast is an ML service for forecasting, CloudWatch tracks operational metrics.

questions.prompt: Who is the main point of contact for billing or account questions if a user has an AWS account with an Enterprise-level AWS Support plan?
questions.explanation: Enterprise Support customers have the AWS Concierge Support Team as their primary point of contact for billing and account-level questions.

questions.prompt: What is one technique to provide unified billing if each department within a firm has its own AWS account?

options.text: Use AWS Budgets on each account to pay only to budget.
Contact AWS Support for a monthly bill.
Create an AWS Organization from the payer account and invite the other accounts to join.
Put all invoices into one Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) bucket, load data into Amazon Redshift, and then run a billing report.

questions.explanation: Consolidated billing through AWS Organizations rolls all member accounts into a single payer account, producing one invoice and aggregating usage to reach tiered-pricing discounts sooner.

questions.prompt: Which cloud architecture design concept is supported by distributing workloads across various Availability Zones?
questions.explanation: Spreading workloads across Availability Zones is the canonical implementation of the "Design for failure" principle — assume any one AZ can fail and the workload still serves users.

questions.prompt: Which security-related duty is AWS accountable for under the AWS shared responsibility model?
questions.explanation: Physical security of AWS data centers — access control, environmental safeguards, hardware decommissioning — is AWS's responsibility under the shared model. IAM lifecycle, EBS encryption choice, and security group rules are the customer's.

questions.prompt: A company wants to migrate a critical application to AWS. The application has a short runtime. The application is invoked by changes in data or by shifts in system state. The company needs a compute solution that maximizes operational efficiency and minimizes the cost of running the application. Which AWS solution should the company use to meet these requirements?
questions.explanation: AWS Lambda runs event-driven code on demand and bills only for the millisecond-level execution time. For short-running, event-triggered workloads it is the most operationally efficient and cost-effective compute option.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service or feature allows users to connect with and deploy AWS services programmatically?
questions.explanation: The AWS SDKs let applications connect to and deploy AWS services programmatically. The Management Console is a UI, Cloud9 is a browser IDE, CodePipeline is a CI/CD service.

questions.prompt: According to the AWS shared responsibility model, who is responsible for managing IAM user access and secret keys?

options.text: IAM access and secret keys are static, so there is no need to rotate them.
The customer is responsible for rotating keys.
AWS will rotate the keys whenever required.
The AWS Support team will rotate keys when requested by the customer.

questions.explanation: IAM credentials belong to the customer — rotating access keys regularly is the customer's responsibility under the shared model. AWS does not automatically rotate user keys.

questions.prompt: To install a PCI-compliant workload on AWS, which of the following tasks is required?

options.text: Use any AWS service and implement PCI controls at the application layer
Use an AWS service that is in-scope for PCI compliance and raise an AWS support ticket to enable PCI compliance at the application layer
Use any AWS service and raise an AWS support ticket to enable PCI compliance on that service
Use an AWS service that is in scope for PCI compliance and apply PCI controls at the application layer

questions.explanation: PCI compliance on AWS requires using services that are in scope for PCI DSS and then implementing PCI controls at the application layer. AWS will not enable PCI features on individual customer accounts.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a suggestion made by an AWS Trusted Advisor? (Select two.)
questions.explanation: Trusted Advisor's five check categories are Cost Optimization, Performance, Security, Fault Tolerance, and Service Limits. Of the options offered, Cost optimization and Performance match.

questions.prompt: A company plans to create a data lake that uses Amazon S3. Which factor will have the MOST effect on cost?

options.text: The selection of S3 storage tiers
Charges to transfer existing data into Amazon S3
The addition of S3 bucket policies
S3 ingest fees for each request

questions.explanation: S3 storage class selection (Standard, Intelligent-Tiering, Glacier classes, etc.) has by far the largest impact on data-lake cost. Inbound data transfer to S3 is free, bucket policies have no cost, and request fees are usually small relative to storage.

questions.prompt: A company is launching an ecommerce application that must always be available. The application will run on Amazon EC2 instances continuously for the next 12 months. What is the MOST cost-effective instance purchasing option that meets these requirements?
questions.explanation: Savings Plans commit to a dollar-per-hour spend over 1 or 3 years for steep discounts vs. On-Demand — ideal for a workload that must run continuously for 12 months. Spot can be reclaimed (bad for must-always-be-available), Dedicated Hosts are pricier, On-Demand has no commitment but is full list price.

questions.prompt: Which pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework is supported by the design philosophy of performing operations as code?
questions.explanation: "Perform operations as code" is a stated design principle of the Operational Excellence pillar — using scripts and infrastructure-as-code to apply the same DevOps practices to operations that you use for application code.

questions.prompt: What is the customer's responsibility while using Amazon RDS?

options.text: Patching and maintenance of the underlying operating system.
Managing automatic backups of the database.
Controlling network access through security groups.
Replacing failed instances in the event of a hardware failure.

questions.explanation: With RDS, AWS manages the underlying OS, engine patches, backups, and hardware. The customer is responsible for network access (security groups), IAM permissions, and the data itself.

questions.prompt: What is the customer's obligation while using an AWS managed service under the AWS shared responsibility model?

options.text: Physical security of the data centers

questions.explanation: Customer data is always the customer's responsibility regardless of the service model. Physical security, OS patching of managed services, and server-side encryption mechanics are AWS's.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service or feature can a company use to determine which business unit is using specific AWS resources?
questions.explanation: Cost allocation tags attach metadata (e.g., business unit, cost center) to AWS resources so spend can be sliced by tag in Cost Explorer and the Cost & Usage Report.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service or tool should a company use to centrally request and track service limit increases?
questions.explanation: AWS Service Quotas is the central place to view and request increases for service limits across AWS services. Config tracks resource configuration; Service Catalog distributes approved products; Budgets tracks spend.

questions.prompt: Which pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework is designed on the idea of frequent, minor, reversible changes?
questions.explanation: "Make frequent, small, reversible changes" is one of the design principles listed under Operational Excellence.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service can identify the person who made the API request when an Amazon EC2 instance is terminated?
questions.explanation: AWS CloudTrail records every API call, including the IAM principal, source IP, and timestamp — exactly what you need to identify who terminated an instance. CloudWatch is for metrics/logs; X-Ray traces app requests; IAM defines permissions but doesn't log usage.

questions.prompt: All AWS users have access to which AWS Trusted Advisor check?
questions.explanation: All AWS customers (including Basic and Developer support) get the core Trusted Advisor checks (a limited set of security and service-limit checks). The full check suite is available only on Business and Enterprise Support.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an example of security in the AWS Cloud under the AWS shared responsibility model?
questions.explanation: Firewall (security group) configuration is the customer's responsibility — an example of security IN the cloud. Edge locations, physical security, and global infrastructure are AWS's responsibility.

questions.prompt: Which documentation does AWS Artifact provide?

options.text: A history of a company's AWS spending
A list of previous-generation Amazon EC2 instance types

questions.explanation: AWS Artifact is the on-demand portal for compliance reports (SOC, PCI, ISO, FedRAMP) and online agreements such as the BAA.

questions.prompt: Which task requires using AWS account root user credentials?

options.text: Starting and stopping Amazon EC2 instances

questions.explanation: Changing the AWS Support plan is one of the tasks that require root user credentials. Billing viewing, EC2 lifecycle, and opening support cases can all be delegated to IAM users.

questions.prompt: Permissions for which of the following are managed by service control policies (SCPs)?
questions.explanation: Service control policies (SCPs) are an AWS Organizations feature that defines the maximum permissions available to accounts in the organization. They do not manage Regions, AZs, or edge locations directly.

questions.prompt: Which of the following AWS services should a client utilize to audit the change management of AWS resources?
questions.explanation: AWS Config records configuration changes over time and evaluates resources against rules — the right tool for audit of change management. Trusted Advisor offers best-practice checks; CloudWatch is metrics/logs; Inspector scans for vulnerabilities.

questions.prompt: What is raised when a business deploys web servers across several AWS Regions?
questions.explanation: Multi-Region deployments primarily raise availability — surviving the loss of an entire Region. Durability speaks to data integrity, security is broader, coupling is about software design.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a shared control between a client and AWS under the shared responsibility model?
questions.explanation: Patch management is explicitly a shared control: AWS patches the infrastructure for managed services, customers patch guest OSes on EC2 and their own applications.

questions.prompt: What is the scope of a VPC within the AWS network?

options.text: A VPC can span all Availability Zones globally.
A VPC must span at least two subnets in each AWS Region.
A VPC must span at least two edge locations in each AWS Region.
A VPC can span all Availability Zones within an AWS Region.

questions.explanation: A VPC is regional — its subnets can live in any Availability Zone within a single Region, but a VPC itself cannot span multiple Regions.

questions.prompt: Which of the following are components of an AWS Site-to-Site VPN connection? (Choose two.)
questions.explanation: A Site-to-Site VPN has a Virtual Private Gateway on the AWS side and a Customer Gateway representing the on-prem device. Storage Gateway is for hybrid storage; NAT and Internet Gateways serve outbound/inbound VPC internet traffic.

questions.prompt: According to the AWS shared responsibility model, which job is shared between AWS and the customer?
questions.explanation: Patch management and configuration management are both listed by AWS as shared controls. Physical/environmental controls are AWS-only; server hardware management is AWS; application security is customer-only.

questions.prompt: How should an application be created to function on the AWS Cloud in accordance with best practices?
questions.explanation: Loose coupling — components that interact via well-defined interfaces and degrade independently — is a foundational cloud design best practice (and an AWS Well-Architected principle).

questions.prompt: Which cloud architectural concept is supported by a system that can scale in terms of users, traffic, or data quantity without sacrificing performance?
questions.explanation: Elasticity is exactly the ability to scale resources up and down with demand while preserving performance. Decoupling is structural; design for failure is about reliability; "think parallel" is a complementary concept but not the main fit here.

questions.prompt: The following describes an application that spans various Availability Zones:
questions.explanation: Spanning multiple AZs is the canonical pattern for high availability — the workload survives the loss of any single AZ.

questions.prompt: A company needs to establish a connection between two VPCs. The VPCs are located in two different AWS Regions. The company wants to use the existing infrastructure of the VPCs for this connection. Which AWS service or feature can be used to establish this connection?
questions.explanation: Inter-Region VPC peering connects two VPCs across Regions using the AWS backbone with no extra hardware. Client VPN is for end-user access; Direct Connect needs a physical circuit; VPC endpoints connect a VPC to AWS services.

questions.prompt: According to the AWS shared responsibility model, what responsibility does a customer have when using Amazon RDS to host a database?
questions.explanation: With RDS the customer manages database connections (security groups, IAM authentication, app-side connection pools). AWS installs and patches the engine, replaces failed instances, and provides KMS encryption — the customer just opts in.

questions.prompt: Which duty is the customer's responsibility while administering AWS Lambda functions under the AWS shared responsibility model?

options.text: Scaling Lambda resources according to demand

questions.explanation: With Lambda the customer creates and versions function code. AWS owns the servers, OS, scaling decisions, and runtime updates.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not a duty of the client under the AWS shared responsibility model? (Select two.)

options.text: Patch management of an Amazon RDS instance operating system
Controlling physical access to data centers
Patch management of an Amazon EC2 instance operating system

questions.explanation: Decommissioning physical drives and controlling physical access to data centers are both AWS-only duties. Security groups/NACLs and EC2 OS patching are customer duties. RDS OS patching is AWS — though that option is not asked here as a customer duty.

questions.prompt: Which service or functionality does AWS Enterprise Support provide that other AWS Support subscriptions do not?
questions.explanation: The Concierge Team is exclusive to Enterprise Support. Trusted Advisor is in every plan (limited at lower tiers); support cases are available from Developer up; Amazon Connect is unrelated.

questions.prompt: What attributes of an AWS account can AWS Trusted Advisor monitor and advise on? (Select two.)
questions.explanation: Trusted Advisor's check categories are security best practices, cost optimization, performance, fault tolerance, and service limits. Application performance and compliance status are not Trusted Advisor categories.

questions.prompt: Which feature enables Amazon EC2 instances to be more elastic in response to changing workload demand?
questions.explanation: Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling adds and removes instances automatically in response to demand, making the fleet elastic. Resource groups are organizational; lifecycle policies belong to S3/storage; an ALB distributes traffic but does not change capacity.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service or feature acts as a firewall for Amazon EC2 instances?
questions.explanation: Security groups act as stateful, instance-level firewalls. Network ACLs are stateless and operate at the subnet level. A VPC is the network itself; an ENI is just a virtual network card.

questions.prompt: Which feature enables Amazon EC2 instances to be more elastic in response to changing workload demand?
questions.explanation: REVIEW: the source PDF appears to mismatch this prompt with options about shared-responsibility categories. Of the options offered, Virtualization Management is the only AWS-responsibility item present — but none directly answers "makes EC2 more elastic." Treat with care during review.

questions.prompt: What is the unique advantage that Enterprise Support customers receive?

options.text: Access to a Technical Project Manager
Access to a Technical Account Manager
Access to a Cloud Support Engineer

questions.explanation: Enterprise Support is the only tier that includes a designated Technical Account Manager (TAM) as a unique advantage.

questions.prompt: What are the advantages of AWS Cloud elasticity?

options.text: Ensure web traffic is automatically spread across multiple AWS Regions.
Minimize storage costs by automatically archiving log data.
Enable AWS to automatically select the most cost-effective services.
Automatically adjust the required compute capacity to maintain consistent performance.

questions.explanation: Elasticity is the ability to automatically adjust compute capacity (scale out and in) to maintain consistent performance as demand changes.

questions.prompt: Which cost components should be addressed when developing a cloud Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) model for AWS workloads? (Select three.)

options.text: Network infrastructure costs F. Hardware lifecycle costs

questions.explanation: When modeling AWS workload TCO, the major direct AWS cost categories are Compute, Storage, and Data Transfer. Facilities, network infrastructure, and hardware lifecycle are on-premises costs you compare AWS against — not AWS-side cost components.

questions.prompt: Which of the following are capabilities of the AWS Cost Management tools? (Select two.)

options.text: Terminate all AWS resources automatically if budget thresholds are exceeded.
Break down AWS costs by day, service, and linked AWS account.
Create budgets and receive notifications if current or forecasted usage exceeds the budgets.
Switch automatically to Reserved Instances or Spot Instances, whichever is most cost-effective.
Move data stored in Amazon S3 to a more cost-effective storage class.

questions.explanation: Cost Explorer breaks down spend by day, service, and linked account; AWS Budgets sets thresholds and sends notifications. AWS does not automatically terminate workloads on budget overrun, switch purchasing options, or move S3 objects between classes by itself.

questions.prompt: How should an Amazon EC2 instance be granted access to an Amazon S3 bucket in accordance with security best practices?

options.text: Hard code an IAM user's secret key and access key directly in the application, and upload the file
Store the IAM user's secret key and access key in a text file on the EC2 instance, read the keys, then upload the file.
Have the EC2 instance assume a role to obtain the privileges to upload the file.
Modify the S3 bucket policy so that any service can upload to it at any time.

questions.explanation: IAM roles give EC2 temporary credentials via the instance profile — never hard-code keys or read them from a file. Public bucket policies expose the bucket to the world.

questions.prompt: A company has a single Amazon EC2 instance. The company wants to adopt a highly available architecture. What can the company do to meet this requirement?

options.text: Scale vertically to a larger EC2 instance size.
Scale horizontally across multiple Availability Zones.
Change the EC2 instance family to a compute optimized instance.

questions.explanation: Horizontal scaling across multiple Availability Zones is the canonical pattern for high availability. Vertical scaling alone, Dedicated Instances, or compute-optimized families do not address AZ-level resilience.

questions.prompt: A company's on-premises application deployment cycle was 3–4 weeks. After migrating to the AWS Cloud, the company can deploy the application in 2–3 days. Which benefit has this company experienced by moving to the AWS Cloud?
questions.explanation: Going from a 3–4 week deploy cycle to 2–3 days is the textbook example of agility — the ability to deploy faster as a benefit of AWS.

questions.prompt: Which of the following price options would result in the highest reduction after making an Amazon EC2 Dedicated Host reservation?
questions.explanation: Among Dedicated Host reservation payment options, All-Upfront yields the largest discount. Partial-Upfront is next, then No-Upfront. Hourly On-Demand is full list price (no commitment).

questions.prompt: Which AWS services and/or features increase availability and mitigate the effect of failures while constructing a typical three-tier web application? (Select two.)

options.text: AWS Auto Scaling for Amazon EC2 instances
Amazon VPC subnet ACLs to check the health of a service
Distributed resources across multiple Availability Zones
AWS Server Migration Service (AWS SMS) to move Amazon EC2 instances into a different Region
Distributed resources across multiple AWS points of presence

questions.explanation: Auto Scaling replaces unhealthy instances and adjusts to load; spreading across multiple AZs survives any single-AZ failure. NACLs don't health-check; AWS SMS migrates servers; "AWS points of presence" relate to CloudFront, not three-tier app availability.

questions.prompt: Which of the following IT responsibilities may AWS relieve a company's IT team of? (Select two.)

options.text: Setting up access controls for data

questions.explanation: AWS handles database engine patching (for managed services) and storage capacity planning at the infrastructure level. Schema design, access controls, and application code remain customer responsibilities.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is included in the AWS Trusted Advisor checks? (Select two.)

options.text: Information on Amazon S3 bucket permissions
Multi-factor authentication enabled on the AWS account root user
Number of users in the account

questions.explanation: Trusted Advisor's core checks include S3 bucket permissions and "MFA on root account." Service outages live in AWS Health; software patches in Inspector / SSM Patch Manager; user-count is not a standard TA check.

questions.prompt: What is AWS accountable for under the AWS shared responsibility model?
questions.explanation: AWS manages the underlying network infrastructure. VPC configuration, application code, and the application's traffic patterns are customer responsibilities.

questions.prompt: Which of the following tasks may be used to improve Amazon EC2 costs? (Select two.)

options.text: Implementing Auto Scaling groups to add and remove instances based on demand.
Creating a policy to restrict IAM users from creating new instances.
Setting a budget to limit spending on EC2 instances using AWS Budgets.
Adding EC2 instances to a second AWS Region that is geographically close to the end users.

questions.explanation: Auto Scaling removes idle capacity; Reserved Instances trade commitment for steep discounts. Budgets only surface spend; IAM restrictions don't reduce existing instance cost; placing instances in a second Region adds cost.

questions.prompt: Which of the following are included in AWS Enterprise Support? (Choose two.)

options.text: Support of third-party software integration to AWS

questions.explanation: Enterprise Support includes a designated Technical Account Manager and support for third-party software integration. Professional Services is a separate paid offering; partner-led support is a different program; the critical-incident response target is 15 minutes (not 5).

questions.prompt: A company wants to limit its employees' AWS access to a portfolio of predefined AWS resources. Which AWS solution should the company use to meet this requirement?
questions.explanation: AWS Service Catalog lets administrators create curated portfolios of approved AWS products that end users can launch, restricting them to a vetted set of resources.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a customer duty under the AWS shared responsibility model?

options.text: Installing security patches for the Xen and KVM hypervisors
Installing operating system patches for Amazon DynamoDB
Installing operating system security patches for Amazon EC2 database instances
Installing operating system security patches for Amazon RDS database instances

questions.explanation: Customers patch the guest OS on their EC2 instances (including EC2-hosted databases). AWS patches DynamoDB, the hypervisor, and the OS for managed services like RDS.

questions.prompt: A firm wishes to develop a new line of business application. Which design concepts should be applied in accordance with the AWS Well- Architected Framework? (Select two.)

options.text: Consolidate multiple AWS accounts into a single account.
Buy and host hardware in the AWS Cloud.
Decouple the AWS Cloud architecture to break up monolithic deployments.
Move on-premises network hardware to VPCs.
Design elasticity into the AWS Cloud design.

questions.explanation: The Well-Architected Framework explicitly recommends decoupling components (microservices, queues, events) and designing for elasticity. Consolidating accounts, buying hardware to host in AWS, and migrating physical network gear are anti-patterns.

questions.prompt: Which scenarios should a user report to the AWS Abuse team? (Select two.)

options.text: A DDoS attack is being made on an AWS resource.
A SQL injection attack is being made from an IP address that is not an AWS address.
AWS resources are being used to host objectionable or illegal content.
A company's resources are being used in a way that is inconsistent with corporate policy.
A company is receiving HTTPS requests on a web server that is serving HTTP.

questions.explanation: Report DDoS attacks against AWS resources and AWS resources being used to host illegal/objectionable content to the AWS Abuse team. SQL injection from non-AWS IPs is a customer-side detection problem; internal policy violations and HTTP/HTTPS mismatches aren't abuse-team matters.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is the customer's obligation under the AWS shared responsibility model?

options.text: Patching and fixing flaws in the infrastructure

questions.explanation: Patching guest OS and applications is the customer's obligation. Infrastructure patching, physical/environmental controls, and AWS infrastructure device configuration are AWS responsibilities.

questions.prompt: An online company was running a workload on premises and was struggling to launch new products and features. After migrating the workload to AWS, the company can quickly launch products and features and can scale its infrastructure as required. Which AWS Cloud value proposition does this scenario describe?
questions.explanation: Faster product launches and the ability to scale infrastructure on demand are the defining benefits of business agility on the cloud.

questions.prompt: Which of the following are advantages of the AWS Cloud? (Choose two.)

options.text: Ability to quickly change required capacity

questions.explanation: Quick capacity change (elasticity) and high economies of scale are stated AWS Cloud advantages. AWS doesn't manage customer infrastructure; deployment time decreases, not increases; fixed costs are replaced by variable costs.

questions.prompt: AWS has the ability to achieve lower pay-as-you-go pricing by aggregating usage across hundreds of thousands of users. This describes which advantage of the AWS Cloud?
questions.explanation: Aggregating demand across many customers produces massive economies of scale that AWS passes back as lower per-unit pricing.

questions.prompt: Which AWS serverless platform services are included?

options.text: Amazon EC2, Amazon S3, Amazon Athena
Amazon Kinesis, Amazon SOS, Amazon EMR
AWS Step Functions, Amazon DynamoDB, Amazon SNS
Amazon Athena, Amazon Cognito, Amazon EC2

questions.explanation: Step Functions, DynamoDB, and SNS are all serverless (no servers to provision). Options A, B, and D include EC2 or EMR, which require server management.

questions.prompt: According to the AWS shared responsibility model, which of the following is the customer’s obligation?
questions.explanation: Patching Amazon EC2 (guest OS) is the customer's responsibility. Infrastructure patching, physical security, and network-infrastructure patching are AWS responsibilities.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is the customer’s obligation under the shared responsibility model?

options.text: Ensuring that disk drives are wiped after use
Ensuring that firmware is updated on hardware devices
Ensuring that data is encrypted at rest
Ensuring that network cables are category six or higher

questions.explanation: Ensuring data is encrypted at rest is the customer's choice and obligation (customer enables encryption and manages keys). Disk wiping, firmware updates, and cabling specs are AWS responsibilities.

questions.prompt: What is the AWS Cloud’s value proposition?

options.text: AWS is responsible for security in the AWS Cloud
AWS manages user applications in the AWS Cloud

questions.explanation: AWS Cloud's value proposition includes no long-term contracts — pay-as-you-go. Security is shared (not solely AWS "in" the cloud); new servers in minutes (not days); customers manage their own applications.

questions.prompt: A company has a database server that is always running. The company hosts the server on Amazon EC2 instances. The instance sizes are suitable for the workload. The workload will run for 1 year. Which EC2 instance purchasing option will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively?
questions.explanation: For an always-on, fixed-size workload running 1 year, Standard Reserved Instances give the deepest discount over On-Demand. Spot can be reclaimed (unsuitable for an always-on DB); Convertible RIs trade discount for flexibility.

questions.prompt: Which tasks are the responsibility of AWS, according to the AWS shared responsibility model? (Choose two.)

options.text: Patch the Amazon EC2 guest operating system
Upgrade the firmware of the network infrastructure
Apply password rotation for IAM users
Maintain the physical security of edge locations
Maintain least privilege access to the root user account

questions.explanation: AWS upgrades the firmware of the underlying network infrastructure and maintains the physical security of edge locations. EC2 OS patching, IAM password rotation, and protecting root account access are customer duties.

questions.prompt: Which of the following are features of network ACLs as they are used in the AWS Cloud? (Choose two.)

options.text: They evaluate all rules before allowing traffic
They process rules in order, starting with the lowest numbered rule, when deciding whether to allow traffic
They operate at the instance level

questions.explanation: Network ACLs are stateless and process rules in order, starting with the lowest-numbered rule, until one matches. They operate at the subnet level, not the instance level (that's security groups, which are stateful).

questions.prompt: A company has designed its AWS Cloud infrastructure to run its workloads effectively. The company also has protocols in place to continuously improve supporting processes. Which pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework does this scenario represent?
questions.explanation: Running workloads effectively and continuously improving supporting processes is the description of the Operational Excellence pillar.

questions.prompt: Which of the following describes elasticity in the AWS Cloud? (Select two.)

options.text: How quickly an Amazon EC2 instance can be restarted
The ability to right-size resources as demand shifts
The maximum amount of RAM an Amazon EC2 instance can use
How easily resources can be produced when they are needed

questions.explanation: Elasticity is the ability to right-size and easily produce resources when needed. Instance restart time, max RAM, and pricing model are unrelated definitions.

questions.prompt: After studying the AWS Cost and Usage report in the AWS Management Console, a Cloud Practitioner detects a billing problem. Which course of action should be done to rectify this?

options.text: Open a detailed case related to billing and submit it to AWS Support for help
Upload data describing the issue to a new object in a private Amazon S3 bucket
Create a pricing application and deploy it to a right-sized Amazon EC2 instance for more information
Proceed with creating a new dashboard in Amazon QuickSight

questions.explanation: Open a detailed AWS Support case for billing issues. Uploading data to S3, deploying pricing apps, or building a QuickSight dashboard don't engage the people who can fix billing problems.

questions.prompt: Which action, in terms of AWS Cloud architecture, is compatible with the concept of least privilege?

options.text: Allow users the minimum access that is needed to do a task
Prevent managers from accessing important source code
Provide only the permissions that are needed for users to do their jobs in the current month
Assign permissions that are based on job titles

questions.explanation: Least privilege grants users the minimum access needed to complete their tasks. Time-limited ("current month") and job-title-based assignment are role-based, not least-privilege concepts.

questions.prompt: Which of the following are customer duties under the AWS shared responsibility model? (Select two.)

options.text: Setting up server-side encryption on an Amazon S3 bucket
Physical security of data center facilities

questions.explanation: Setting up S3 server-side encryption (the customer enables it and chooses keys) and configuring network/firewall rules are customer duties. RDS OS patching, physical data-center security, and compute capacity are AWS's.

questions.prompt: A company needs to graphically visualize AWS billing and usage over time. The company also needs information about its AWS monthly costs. Which AWS Billing and Cost Management tool provides this data in a graphical format?
questions.explanation: AWS Cost Explorer provides graphical visualization of AWS spending and usage over time, plus monthly cost breakdowns. AWS Bills is itemized; CUR is raw data; Budgets is thresholds.

questions.prompt: A company wants to run production workloads on AWS. The company needs concierge service, a designated AWS technical account manager (TAM), and technical support that is available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. Which AWS Support plan will meet these requirements?
questions.explanation: Enterprise Support is the only plan that includes a designated TAM, the Concierge team, and 24/7 technical support.

questions.prompt: Which of the following tasks is the AWS customer’s duty under the shared responsibility model? (Select two.)

options.text: Ensuring that application data is encrypted at rest
Ensuring that AWS NTP servers are set to the correct time
Ensuring that users have received security training in the use of AWS services
Ensuring that access to data centers is restricted
Ensuring that hardware is disposed of properly

questions.explanation: Encrypting application data at rest and ensuring AWS-user security training are customer duties. NTP servers, data-center physical access, and hardware disposal are AWS responsibilities.

questions.prompt: Before transferring an environment to the AWS Cloud, a cloud practitioner must receive AWS compliance reports. How are these reports produced?

options.text: Download the reports from AWS Artifact
Open a case with AWS Support
Generate the reports with Amazon Macie

questions.explanation: Compliance reports (SOC, PCI, ISO, FedRAMP) are downloaded on-demand from AWS Artifact.

questions.prompt: Which change management actions, according to the AWS Well-Architected Framework, should be followed to ensure AWS Cloud reliability? (Select two.)

options.text: Use AWS Config to generate an inventory of AWS resources
Use service limits to prevent users from creating or making changes to AWS resources
Use AWS CloudTrail to record AWS API calls into an auditable log file
Use AWS Certificate Manager to whitelist approved AWS resources and services
Use Amazon GuardDuty to validate configuration changes made to AWS resources

questions.explanation: AWS Config maintains an inventory and tracks configuration changes; CloudTrail records auditable logs of API calls. Both are core Reliability/operational-excellence change-management controls.

questions.prompt: What may be associated to an Amazon EC2 instance through AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) to initiate service requests?
questions.explanation: IAM roles (attached via an instance profile) let EC2 instances make AWS service calls with temporary credentials. Groups apply to users, policies are attached to identities, access keys belong to IAM users.

questions.prompt: Which of the following actions is the customer’s responsibility under the AWS shared responsibility model? (Select two.)

options.text: Patching operating system components for Amazon Relational Database Server (Amazon RDS)
Maintaining environmental controls within a data center

questions.explanation: Client-side encryption and configuring Network ACLs are customer responsibilities. RDS OS patching, data-center staff training, and environmental controls are AWS responsibilities.

questions.prompt: Which architecture design principle describes the need to isolate failures between dependent components in the AWS Cloud?

options.text: Design for single points of failure

questions.explanation: Loose coupling — components communicating via well-defined contracts so one failure doesn't cascade — is the cloud design principle that isolates failures between dependent components.

questions.prompt: Which AWS services are managed database services? (Choose two.)
questions.explanation: Amazon RDS and DynamoDB are managed database services. EBS is block storage, S3 is object storage, EFS is file storage.

questions.prompt: Which AWS services or functionalities enable failover across several AWS Regions to ensure high availability and low latency? (Select two.)
questions.explanation: Route 53 health-check / latency / failover routing and AWS Global Accelerator both work across Regions. ALB and NLB are regional load balancers; S3 Transfer Acceleration accelerates uploads, not failover.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a duty of AWS under the AWS shared responsibility model?

options.text: Enabling server-side encryption for objects stored in S3
Patching the operating system on an Amazon EC2 instance

questions.explanation: AWS applies updates to the hypervisor and underlying infrastructure. Enabling S3 SSE, applying IAM policies, and patching the EC2 OS are customer duties.

questions.prompt: A copy of the AWS SOC 2 report is required during a compliance review by one of the auditors. Which service should this request be sent through?
questions.explanation: AWS Artifact is the on-demand portal for the AWS SOC 2 report and other compliance documentation. Personal Health Dashboard is for service health; Trusted Advisor is best-practice checks; S3 is object storage.

questions.prompt: For 3 hours, 5 minutes, and 6 seconds, a client operates an Amazon Linux EC2 instance on-demand. How long will the consumer be charged?
questions.explanation: Amazon Linux On-Demand EC2 instances bill per second (with a 60-second minimum). 3 hours, 5 minutes, 6 seconds bills exactly as 3 hours, 5 minutes, and 6 seconds.

questions.prompt: A company is using the AWS Free Tier for several AWS services for an application. What will happen if the Free Tier usage period expires or if the application use exceeds the Free Tier usage limits?

options.text: The company will be charged the standard pay-as-you-go service rates for the usage that exceeds the Free Tier usage.
AWS Support will contact the company to set up standard service charges.
The company will be charged for the services it consumed during the Free Tier period, plus additional charges for service consumption after the Free Tier period.
The company’s AWS account will be frozen and can be restarted after a payment plan is established.

questions.explanation: If Free Tier limits are exceeded or the 12-month period ends, AWS charges the standard pay-as-you-go rates for the overage. No retroactive billing, no frozen account, no contact required.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service uses machine learning to help discover, monitor, and protect sensitive data that is stored in Amazon S3 buckets?
questions.explanation: Amazon Macie uses ML to discover, classify, and monitor sensitive data (PII, PHI, financial data) in S3. Shield is DDoS protection, Network Firewall is L3/L4 filtering, Cognito is app user identity.

questions.prompt: According to the AWS shared responsibility model, which of the following is exclusively the responsibility of AWS?

options.text: Patching of the guest operating system
Development of an IAM password policy

questions.explanation: Physical and environmental controls are AWS-exclusive responsibilities. Guest OS patching, IAM password policy, and security awareness/training are customer (or shared) duties.

questions.prompt: What can users do using AWS Marketplace? (Select two.)

options.text: Sell unused Amazon EC2 Spot Instances.
Sell solutions to other AWS users.
Buy third-party software that runs on AWS.
Purchase AWS security and compliance documents.

questions.explanation: AWS Marketplace lets you both sell solutions to other AWS users and buy third-party software that runs on AWS. Spot Instances cannot be resold today, compliance documents live in AWS Artifact, and Snowball is ordered through the console.

questions.prompt: What are the possible uses for AWS edge locations? (Select two.)

options.text: Reducing traffic on the server by caching responses
Sending notification messages to end users

questions.explanation: Edge locations (used by CloudFront) deliver content closer to viewers and cache responses to reduce origin traffic. They are not for application hosting, NoSQL caches, or sending notifications.

questions.prompt: Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) and Amazon DynamoDB are used by a firm to execute its mission-critical web application. Multiple times during the day, the workload jumps to up to ten times the regular level. Which AWS Cloud feature helps the business to adapt to these demand changes?
questions.explanation: Handling sudden 10× spikes is the textbook definition of scalability — the ability to grow capacity to match demand.

questions.prompt: A company wants to improve the overall availability and performance of its applications that are hosted on AWS. Which AWS service should the company use?
questions.explanation: AWS Global Accelerator routes traffic to optimal regional endpoints over the AWS backbone, improving both availability (health-check based failover) and performance (lower-latency anycast IPs).

questions.prompt: Which of the following tasks is the customer’s duty under the shared responsibility model? (Select two.)

options.text: Maintaining the underlying Amazon EC2 hardware.
Managing the VPC network access control lists.
Encrypting data in transit and at rest.
Deploying hardware in different Availability Zones.

questions.explanation: Managing VPC Network ACLs and encrypting data in transit and at rest are customer duties. EC2 hardware, failed disks, and AZ hardware deployment are AWS responsibilities.

questions.prompt: Which of the following are AWS obligations, according to the AWS shared responsibility model? (Select two.)

options.text: Network infrastructure and virtualization of infrastructure

questions.explanation: AWS owns the network infrastructure and virtualization layer and physical security of hardware. Application data, guest OS, and customer credentials/policies are customer responsibilities.

questions.prompt: What charges are included in the comparison of AWS vs on-premises Total Cost of Ownership (TCO)?
questions.explanation: Operating system administration is a TCO line item compared between on-prem (customer staffs and licenses) and AWS (largely covered by managed services). Project management and business analysis are not standard TCO comparison items.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service or feature identifies whether an Amazon S3 bucket or an IAM role has been shared with an external entity?
questions.explanation: AWS IAM Access Analyzer identifies resources (S3 buckets, IAM roles, KMS keys, Lambda functions, SQS queues, Secrets Manager secrets) shared with external entities.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service supports MySQL and PostgreSQL as relational databases?
questions.explanation: Amazon Aurora is the AWS managed relational engine that is MySQL- and PostgreSQL-compatible. Redshift is a data warehouse, DynamoDB is NoSQL, Neptune is a graph database.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service is natively supported by AWS Snowball Edge?
questions.explanation: AWS Snowball Edge can run a small set of Amazon EC2 instance types locally on the device for on-prem compute alongside data transfer. SMS, Aurora, and Trusted Advisor are not Snowball Edge features.

questions.prompt: Which AWS shared responsibility controls are shared? (Select two.)

options.text: Service and communications protection or security

questions.explanation: AWS lists three shared controls: Patch Management, Configuration Management, and Awareness & Training. RDS patching is AWS-only, physical/environmental are AWS-only, service/communications protection is customer.

questions.prompt: A company does not want to rely on elaborate forecasting to determine its usage of compute resources. Instead, the company wants to pay only for the resources that it uses. The company also needs the ability to increase or decrease its resource usage to meet business requirements. Which pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework aligns with these requirements?
questions.explanation: Paying only for what you use and scaling resource usage to match demand are core to the Cost Optimization pillar.

questions.prompt: After a single Availability Zone service disruption, a corporation must guarantee that the endpoint for a database instance stays the same. The program must continue database operations without human intervention from an administrator. How are these stipulations to be met?

options.text: Use multiple Amazon Route 53 routes to the standby database instance endpoint hosted on AWS Storage Gateway.
Configure Amazon RDS Multi-Availability Zone deployments with automatic failover to the standby.
Add multiple Application Load Balancers and deploy the database instance with AWS Elastic Beanstalk.
Deploy a single Network Load Balancer to distribute incoming traffic across multiple Amazon CloudFront origins.
