questions.explanation: Amazon RDS Multi-AZ deployments automatically fail over to a standby in another AZ with the same endpoint, requiring no admin action. The other options misuse Route 53, ALBs, or NLBs in ways that don't preserve a database endpoint.

questions.prompt: Which costs must be addressed when comparing AWS Cloud vs on-premises Total Cost of Ownership? (Select two.)
questions.explanation: Storage hardware and physical servers are the canonical infrastructure cost lines compared between on-prem and AWS TCO. Software development, project management, and antivirus licenses are not infrastructure costs.

questions.prompt: A company is migrating to the AWS Cloud. The company requires consultative review and guidance for its applications during the migration. After the migration is complete, the company requires a response within 30 minutes if business-critical systems go down. Which AWS Support plans meet these requirements? (Choose two.)
questions.explanation: Enterprise Support has a 15-minute critical response target; Enterprise On-Ramp has 30 minutes — both meet "within 30 minutes." Business Support's critical target is 1 hour, which does not. Both Enterprise tiers also include consultative migration guidance.

questions.prompt: Which AWS products anticipate future AWS expenses automatically?

options.text: AWS Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) Calculator

questions.explanation: AWS Cost Explorer includes a forecast feature that anticipates future AWS expenses based on historical usage.

questions.prompt: Which functionality may be utilized to prevent inadvertent overwrites or deletions of Amazon S3 buckets?
questions.explanation: S3 Versioning keeps every version of an object, so accidental overwrites and deletes can be recovered by restoring the prior version.

questions.prompt: Amazon DynamoDB is used by a business in its AWS Cloud architecture. Which of the following is a duty of the organization, according to the AWS shared responsibility model? (Select two.)

options.text: Application of appropriate permissions with IAM tools

questions.explanation: With DynamoDB the customer manages IAM permissions on tables and the choice of encryption options. AWS handles OS patching and infrastructure provisioning/maintenance.

questions.prompt: A company wants to launch its workload on AWS and requires the system to automatically recover from failure. Which pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework includes this requirement?
questions.explanation: Automatically recovering from failure is a stated design principle of the Reliability pillar.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service should a company use to create a NoSQL database?
questions.explanation: Amazon DynamoDB is the AWS managed NoSQL key-value/document database. Aurora is relational, Redshift is a data warehouse, Neptune is a graph database.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service or functionality is utilized by distributed applications to send text and email messages?
questions.explanation: Amazon SNS is a pub/sub service that can deliver messages over SMS, email, push, HTTP, SQS, and Lambda from a single topic. SES is email-only; CloudWatch alarms trigger but don't directly deliver SMS+email; SQS is queues.

questions.prompt: To achieve high availability, how many Availability Zones should computing resources be provided across?

options.text: A minimum of four or more

questions.explanation: High availability on AWS requires distributing across a minimum of two Availability Zones so the workload survives any single-AZ failure.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is AWS's obligation under the AWS shared responsibility model?

options.text: Operating system patching on Amazon EC2 instances

questions.explanation: Firmware updates on hardware are AWS's obligation. Encryption in transit, OS patching of EC2, and encryption at rest are customer responsibilities.

questions.prompt: To boost availability, a user intends to create two more Amazon EC2 instances. What should the user do?

options.text: Launch the instances across multiple Availability Zones in a single AWS Region.
Launch the instances as EC2 Reserved Instances in the same AWS Region and the same Availability Zone.
Launch the instances in multiple AWS Regions, but in the same Availability Zone.
Launch the instances as EC2 Spot Instances in the same AWS Region, but in different Availability Zones.

questions.explanation: Launching the additional instances in multiple AZs within a single Region increases availability — survives any single-AZ outage. Same-AZ defeats the purpose; cross-Region adds latency and complexity for a simple HA pattern.

questions.prompt: What are the customer's duties under the AWS shared responsibility model? (Select two.)
questions.explanation: Data-in-transit security and data-integrity authentication are customer responsibilities. Physical security, physical network devices, and storage device decommissioning are AWS responsibilities.

questions.prompt: A large enterprise with multiple VPCs in several AWS Regions around the world needs to connect and centrally manage network connectivity between its VPCs. Which AWS service or feature meets these requirements?
questions.explanation: AWS Transit Gateway is the central hub that interconnects many VPCs (and on-prem networks) across Regions, replacing meshes of VPC peerings. Direct Connect is the physical circuit, Site-to-Site VPN is one-to-one over the internet, VPC endpoints connect a VPC to AWS services.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service should be used to monitor Amazon EC2 instances for CPU and network utilization?
questions.explanation: Amazon CloudWatch is the metrics + logs service used to monitor EC2 CPU and network utilization. Inspector scans for vulnerabilities, CloudTrail records API calls, Config tracks configuration.

questions.prompt: How can customers minimize the amount of time they spend patching their operating systems by migrating to the AWS Cloud? (Select two.)

options.text: Users can take advantage of managed services on AWS.
Users can outsource operating system patching to the AWS Support team.
AWS Professional Services will upgrade instances to the latest operating system versions.
Users have the ability to use license-included Amazon EC2 instances.
Users can take advantage of AWS Systems Manager features.

questions.explanation: Adopting managed services shifts OS patching to AWS, and AWS Systems Manager (Patch Manager) automates patching of customer-managed instances. AWS Support doesn't patch customer instances, Professional Services is paid consulting, license-included pricing doesn't reduce patching effort.

questions.prompt: In the case of an environmental disruption, a company needs to make sure its infrastructure is structured for fault tolerance and business continuity. Which parts of the AWS architecture should the organization replicate?
questions.explanation: To withstand an environmental disruption (data center / power / cooling event), replicate across Availability Zones — each AZ is a discrete data center campus. Edge locations are caches, Regions are bigger units, Route 53 is DNS.

questions.prompt: According to which AWS cloud design guideline, systems should minimize their interdependence?
questions.explanation: Loose coupling minimizes interdependence between components, letting them evolve and fail independently.

questions.prompt: Which tasks need the root user credentials for an AWS account? (Select two.)

options.text: Creating an Amazon EC2 key pair
Removing an IAM user from the administrators group
Creating an Amazon CloudFront key pair
Granting an IAM user full administrative access

questions.explanation: Changing the AWS Support plan and creating a CloudFront key pair both require the root user. EC2 key pairs and IAM group/permissions changes can be done by IAM users with the right permissions.

questions.prompt: A user wants to deploy a service to the AWS Cloud by using infrastructure-as-code (IaC) principles. Which AWS service can be used to meet this requirement?
questions.explanation: AWS CloudFormation provisions AWS resources from declarative templates — the canonical IaC service. Systems Manager is operations, CodeCommit is Git hosting, Config tracks configuration.

questions.prompt: A company that has multiple business units wants to centrally manage and govern its AWS Cloud environments. The company wants to automate the creation of AWS accounts, apply service control policies (SCPs), and simplify billing processes. Which AWS service or tool should the company use to meet these requirements?
questions.explanation: AWS Organizations centrally manages multiple accounts, applies SCPs, automates account creation, and provides consolidated billing across business units.

questions.prompt: An administrator must fast install and begin utilizing a popular IT product. What resources are available to the administrator?
questions.explanation: AWS Quick Start reference deployments are vetted CloudFormation templates that install popular technologies and architectures rapidly. CloudFront, CodeCommit, and Well-Architected docs don't deploy IT products.

questions.prompt: Which solution enables users in various AWS Regions to have the FASTEST application response times for frequently requested data?

options.text: AWS CloudTrail across multiple Availability Zones
A virtual private gateway over AWS Direct Connect

questions.explanation: CloudFront's global edge-location network caches frequently requested data near users, providing the lowest-latency response. CloudTrail is for audit logs, CloudFormation provisions resources, Direct Connect is a private network link.

questions.prompt: Which qualities make AWS Cloud computing advantageous? (Select two.)

options.text: A 100% service level agreement (SLA) for all AWS services
Compute capacity that is adjusted on demand
Availability of AWS Support for code development

questions.explanation: On-demand compute capacity and enhanced security baseline are stated AWS Cloud benefits. AWS publishes service-specific SLAs (none 100%), code development isn't a primary Support benefit, and cost/complexity are reduced not increased.

questions.prompt: A corporation anticipates a brief increase in internet traffic for their application. The program cannot be interrupted during the traffic spike. In addition, the organization must reduce costs while increasing flexibility. To achieve these needs, which Amazon EC2 instance type should the organization use?
questions.explanation: On-Demand Instances handle short, no-interruption spikes without commitment — flexible and pay-as-you-go. Spot can be reclaimed; Reserved Instances/Dedicated Hosts require commitment.

questions.prompt: Which IT controls do AWS and the customer share, according to the AWS shared responsibility model? (Choose two.)
questions.explanation: AWS lists Patch Management and Cloud Awareness & Training as shared controls (along with Configuration Management). Physical/environmental and zone security are AWS-only; application data encryption is customer-only.

questions.prompt: A company is launching an application in the AWS Cloud. The application will use Amazon S3 storage. A large team of researchers will have shared access to the data. The company must be able to recover data that is accidentally overwritten or deleted. Which S3 feature should the company turn on to meet this requirement?

options.text: Encryption in transit and at rest

questions.explanation: S3 Versioning is the feature that retains every object version, allowing recovery from accidental overwrites and deletes by shared users.

questions.prompt: An Amazon RDS database instance is deployed across several Availability Zones. Which pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework is included in this strategy?
questions.explanation: Deploying RDS across multiple AZs is a Reliability-pillar pattern — it provides automatic failover to a standby in another AZ when the primary fails.

questions.prompt: Amazon EC2, an Elastic Load Balancer, and Amazon RDS are all components of an architectural design. What is the BEST method for estimating the monthly cost of this architecture?

options.text: Open an AWS Support case, provide the architecture proposal, and ask for a monthly cost estimation.
Collect the published prices of the AWS services and calculate the monthly estimate.
Use the AWS Simple Monthly Calculator to estimate the monthly cost.
Use the AWS Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) Calculator to estimate the monthly cost.

questions.explanation: The AWS Simple Monthly Calculator (today known as AWS Pricing Calculator) lets architects estimate the monthly cost of an architecture before deployment. Support cases aren't for cost estimates; manually summing prices is error-prone; TCO Calculator compares against on-prem, not estimates AWS-only spend.

questions.prompt: According to the AWS shared responsibility model, which job is the customer's duty?

options.text: Maintain the security of the AWS Cloud.
Patch the operating system of Amazon RDS instances.

questions.explanation: Configuring firewalls and networks (security groups, NACLs) is the customer's duty. AWS secures the cloud infrastructure, patches RDS, and runs physical controls.

questions.prompt: On Amazon EC2, a business hosts a web application in a Docker container. Which of the following duties is AWS in charge of?

options.text: Scaling the web application and services developed with Docker
Provisioning or scheduling containers to run on clusters and maintain their availability
Performing hardware maintenance in the AWS facilities that run the AWS Cloud
Managing the guest operating system, including updates and security patches

questions.explanation: With Docker on EC2, AWS performs hardware maintenance in its facilities. The customer scales containers, schedules them, manages availability, and manages the guest OS.

questions.prompt: What are the AWS Cloud's advantages? (Select two.)

options.text: No need to guess capacity requirements
Physical access to cloud data centers

questions.explanation: "Stop guessing capacity" and increased speed-to-market are stated cloud benefits. Pricing is variable not fixed, upfront capex is reduced, physical data-center access is never provided.

questions.prompt: An Elastic Load Balancer, numerous Amazon EC2 instances, and Amazon RDS are used to run a web application on AWS. Which security measures are AWS's responsibility? (Select two.)

options.text: Running a virus scan on EC2 instances
Protecting against IP spoofing and packet sniffing
Installing the latest security patches on the RDS instance
Encrypting communication between the EC2 instances and the Elastic Load Balancer
Configuring a security group and a network access control list (NACL) for EC2 instances

questions.explanation: AWS protects the network against IP spoofing and packet sniffing at the hypervisor/network layer, and AWS installs OS-level security patches on RDS (a managed service). Virus scans, app-to-ELB encryption choices, and security-group rules are customer duties.

questions.prompt: A manufacturing company has a critical application that runs at a remote site that has a slow internet connection. The company wants to migrate the workload to AWS. The application is sensitive to latency and interruptions in connectivity. The company wants a solution that can host this application with minimum latency. Which AWS service or feature should the company use to meet these requirements?
questions.explanation: AWS Outposts brings AWS infrastructure on-premises, hosting AWS services with minimum latency at a remote site where internet is poor and the app is latency/connectivity sensitive. Local Zones are AWS-managed near metro areas; Wavelength is for 5G edges.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service or feature facilitates the purchase and deployment of third-party software by providing an online, managed software catalog?

options.text: Amazon EC2 private Amazon Machine Images (AMls)

questions.explanation: AWS Marketplace is the online catalog for buying, deploying, and metering third-party software that runs on AWS.

questions.prompt: A business wishes to improve its capacity for infrastructure recovery in the event of a natural catastrophe. This capability corresponds to which pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework?
questions.explanation: Infrastructure recovery from a disaster aligns with the Reliability pillar — recovering from disruptions is one of its stated outcomes.

questions.prompt: A business may be required to operate its workload exclusively in its on-premises data center due to performance and regulatory limitations. Which Amazon Web Services (AWS) services or resources should the business utilize? (Select two.)
questions.explanation: AWS Outposts (managed AWS rack on-prem) and Snowball Edge (compute + storage device) both let a business run AWS workloads exclusively on-premises. Pinpoint, WorkLink, and AppSync are not on-prem deployment vehicles.

questions.prompt: For security reasons, a business demands an isolated environment inside AWS. Which course of action is necessary to achieve this?

options.text: Create a separate Availability Zone to host the resources.
Create a separate VPC to host the resources.
Create a placement group to host the resources.
Create an AWS Direct Connect connection between the company and AWS.

questions.explanation: A separate VPC provides an isolated virtual network within AWS. AZs are infrastructure, placement groups affect EC2 placement, Direct Connect is a private link to on-prem.

questions.prompt: How do Amazon's massive economies of scale help customers?

options.text: Periodic price reductions as the result of Amazon's operational efficiencies
New Amazon EC2 instance types providing the latest hardware
The ability to scale up and down when needed
Increased reliability in the underlying hardware of Amazon EC2 instances

questions.explanation: AWS aggregating demand across hundreds of thousands of customers produces operational efficiencies and economies of scale that are passed back to customers as periodic price reductions.

questions.prompt: A company wants to implement threat detection on its AWS infrastructure. However, the company does not want to deploy additional software. Which AWS service should the company use to meet these requirements?
questions.explanation: Amazon GuardDuty is an agentless, ML-driven threat detection service that monitors CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs, and DNS logs — no software deployment required.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service uses edge locations?
questions.explanation: AWS Global Accelerator uses the AWS edge network (anycast IPs at edge locations) to route user traffic to regional endpoints. Aurora and Connect are regional; Outposts is on-prem.

questions.prompt: When utilizing the AWS Command Line Interface (AWS CLI), which of the following Identity and Access Management (IAM) entities is connected with an access key ID and secret access key?
questions.explanation: IAM users have long-term access key IDs and secret access keys used for programmatic access (e.g., CLI). Roles provide temporary credentials, groups/policies have no credentials.

questions.prompt: What is AWS's sole obligation under the AWS shared responsibility model?
questions.explanation: Edge location management is AWS-only. Application security is customer-only; patch management is shared; client-side data is customer-only.

questions.prompt: Which component of the AWS architecture permits global computing and storage deployment?
questions.explanation: AWS Regions are the geographic units that let customers deploy compute and storage globally. AZs are within a Region; tags and resource groups are organizational metadata.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a design concept associated with dependability in the AWS Well-Architected Framework?

options.text: Deployment to a single Availability Zone

questions.explanation: "Automatically recover from failure" is a Reliability design principle. Single-AZ deployment is the opposite of reliable; cost optimization belongs to another pillar; ops-as-code is Operational Excellence.

questions.prompt: Which activity is entirely the user's responsibility while executing workloads on AWS?

options.text: Implementing controls to route application traffic

questions.explanation: Implementing controls to route application traffic (security groups, NACLs, route tables, load balancer config) is entirely the customer's responsibility. Infrastructure patching, physical/environmental controls, and underlying infrastructure are AWS responsibilities.

questions.prompt: Which statement best describes the AWS Cloud's agility?

options.text: Agility gives users the ability to host applications in multiple AWS Regions around the world.
Agility gives users the ability to pay upfront to reduce cost.
Agility provides customizable physical hardware at the lowest possible cost.
Agility provides the means for users to provision resources in minutes.

questions.explanation: Agility is the AWS Cloud's ability to let users provision resources in minutes (vs. weeks on-prem) — enabling rapid experimentation and faster delivery.

questions.prompt: Which AWS product or service enables businesses to monitor and classify their expenditure at a precise level?
questions.explanation: Cost allocation tags let you slice AWS spend by any dimension you tag (team, project, environment), giving fine-grained expenditure tracking in Cost Explorer and the CUR.

questions.prompt: A company needs to install an application in a Docker container. Which AWS service eliminates the need to provision and manage the container hosts?

options.text: Amazon FSx for Windows File Server

questions.explanation: AWS Fargate is the serverless compute engine for containers — used with ECS and EKS, it eliminates the need to provision or manage EC2 hosts. FSx is file storage, ECS without Fargate runs on EC2, EC2 is exactly the thing we're avoiding.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service is responsible for monitoring the health of your applications automatically?
questions.explanation: AWS Elastic Beanstalk's enhanced health monitoring automatically watches the deployed application and underlying resources, reporting health to the console. API Gateway monitors itself; Lambda doesn't watch other apps; Config tracks resource configuration.

questions.prompt: A user is developing a service that adheres to the AWS Well-Architected Framework's operational excellence pillar. Which design concept is the user to adhere to?
questions.explanation: "Anticipate failure" is a stated design principle of Operational Excellence. Make small, reversible changes; automate operations; refine procedures often — not the reverse.

questions.prompt: When comparing AWS Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) to on-premises TCO, what charges are included?
questions.explanation: Data center security is a TCO line that AWS provides (built into service prices) and on-prem customers pay for separately. Project management, antivirus licenses, and software development aren't AWS-vs-on-prem TCO comparison items.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service or feature checks access policies and offers actionable recommendations to help users set secure and functional policies?
questions.explanation: AWS IAM Access Analyzer reviews access policies and produces findings + actionable recommendations to make them secure and least-privilege.

questions.prompt: Which design concept is fulfilled by adhering to the AWS Well-Architected Framework's dependability pillar?
questions.explanation: Testing recovery procedures regularly is a Reliability design principle. Vertical scaling, manual recovery, and manual infrastructure changes are anti-patterns.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service or functionality can assist a business in determining if it has publicly accessible Amazon S3 buckets?
questions.explanation: AWS Trusted Advisor has a security check that flags S3 buckets with public read/write permissions, helping identify publicly accessible buckets quickly.

questions.prompt: Seasonal sales surges occur many times a year for an online retailer, most notably during the holidays. At other times of year, demand is lower. The corporation has difficulty forecasting the seasonal increase in infrastructure demand. Which benefits of migrating to the AWS Cloud would be the most beneficial to the business? (Select two.)
questions.explanation: Elasticity (scale up for seasonal peaks, down at quiet times) and pay-as-you-go pricing (no fixed commitment) directly address unpredictable seasonal demand.

questions.prompt: Which concepts of AWS Cloud architecture may assist boost reliability? (Select two.)
questions.explanation: Testing recovery procedures and automatically recovering from failure are both Reliability design principles. Monolithic architecture and "measuring overall efficiency" don't improve reliability; consumption model is Cost Optimization.

questions.prompt: Which sort of storage does Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) and Amazon FSx provide?
questions.explanation: Amazon EFS and Amazon FSx are managed file storage services (NFS for EFS, Windows/Lustre/OpenZFS/NetApp for FSx variants). S3 is object, EBS is block, instance store is ephemeral block.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service enables customers to view AWS compliance control reports on-demand and self-service?
questions.explanation: AWS Artifact is the on-demand, self-service portal for AWS compliance control reports (SOC, ISO, PCI, FedRAMP).

questions.prompt: What may aid in the evaluation of a cloud-based application? (Select two.)
questions.explanation: AWS Trusted Advisor performs automated evaluations across five categories; AWS Professional Services provides human expertise. Both help evaluate cloud-based applications. Systems Manager is for ops, APN partners help but aren't "AWS," Secrets Manager is unrelated.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service enables expense control across numerous AWS accounts effectively?
questions.explanation: AWS Organizations centralizes management of many AWS accounts, including consolidated billing and SCP-based cost/spend controls.

questions.prompt: A company's on-premises servers and the AWS Cloud need a dedicated network connection. Which Amazon Web Services (AWS) service should be used?
questions.explanation: AWS Direct Connect provides a dedicated private network connection between on-prem and AWS — lower jitter, higher bandwidth than internet VPN.

questions.prompt: On-premises resources have been underused by a user. Which AWS Cloud idea is optimal for resolving this issue?
questions.explanation: Elasticity solves under-utilization: in the cloud you release resources you aren't using rather than over-provisioning and idling.

questions.prompt: Which of the following advantages does Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS) provide over conventional database management?

options.text: AWS manages the data stored in Amazon RDS tables.
AWS manages the maintenance of the operating system.
AWS automatically scales up instance types on demand.

questions.explanation: Amazon RDS managed advantage vs. self-managed DBs: AWS handles OS maintenance (and engine patching, backups, hardware). The customer still manages data and DB engine selection.

questions.prompt: Service control policies (SCPs) manage permissions for which of the following?
questions.explanation: Service control policies (SCPs) define the maximum permissions for accounts in AWS Organizations. They don't manage AZs, Regions, or edge locations.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service can be used to encrypt data at rest?
questions.explanation: AWS KMS creates and manages cryptographic keys used to encrypt data at rest in S3, EBS, RDS, and other services. GuardDuty detects threats, Shield protects against DDoS, Security Hub aggregates findings.

questions.prompt: A cloud practitioner has a seldom run data analysis job that can be stopped without causing damage. Which Amazon EC2 purchase option should be utilized to maximize cost savings?
questions.explanation: Spot Instances offer the largest discounts (up to ~90%) for workloads that can tolerate interruption — perfect for a seldom-run data analysis job that can be stopped without damage.

questions.prompt: What are the advantages of AWS Cloud service billing consolidation? (Select two.)

options.text: A minimal additional fee for use
Custom cost and usage budget creation

questions.explanation: Consolidated billing aggregates usage so accounts reach volume-discount tiers sooner and produces one bill for many accounts. There is no extra fee; installment payments aren't part of it; custom budgets are AWS Budgets.

questions.prompt: Which Amazon EC2 instance type is necessary when a user wishes to use their current per-socket, per-core, or per-virtual machine software licenses on a Microsoft Windows server operating on AWS?
questions.explanation: Dedicated Hosts expose the physical sockets/cores/VM count to the customer, enabling Bring-Your-Own-License (BYOL) for per-socket/core/VM-licensed Windows software.

questions.prompt: When users connect to a website with a worldwide consumer base, they report experiencing delay. Which Amazon Web Services (AWS) offering will enhance the user experience by lowering latency?
questions.explanation: Amazon CloudFront caches content at edge locations close to users worldwide, reducing latency for a global audience.

questions.prompt: Which capabilities or services are available for monitoring an AWS account's charges and expenses? (Select two.)

options.text: Billing alerts and Amazon CloudWatch alarms

questions.explanation: The Cost and Usage Report provides detailed cost data; billing alerts via CloudWatch alarms notify when spend crosses thresholds. Product pages, Pricing Calculator, and Price List API are reference data, not monitoring.

questions.prompt: Which service enables users to store data in the Amazon Web Services cloud?
questions.explanation: Amazon EFS is a managed file storage service. Redshift, RDS, and VPC are not generic data storage services in this question's framing.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service or product enables an organization to automate the delivery of application changes?
questions.explanation: AWS CodeDeploy automates the delivery of application changes to EC2, on-prem, and Lambda. AppFlow moves data between SaaS apps, PrivateLink is networking, EKS Distro is a Kubernetes distribution.

questions.prompt: A corporation wishes to minimize the physical footprint of the computing resources used by developers to execute programs. Which service would enable serverless architectures to address this need?
questions.explanation: AWS Lambda is a serverless compute service — no servers to manage. EC2 is virtual machines, DynamoDB is a database, CodeCommit is Git.

questions.prompt: A Load Balancer Elastic enables online traffic to be distributed across multiple:
questions.explanation: Elastic Load Balancing distributes incoming traffic across multiple targets in multiple Availability Zones within a Region. It is not multi-Region by itself.

questions.prompt: What is AWS Storage Gateway's purpose?

options.text: It ensures on-premises data storage is 99.999999999% durable.
It transports petabytes of data to and from AWS.
It connects to multiple Amazon EC2 instances.
It connects on-premises data storage to the AWS Cloud.

questions.explanation: AWS Storage Gateway connects on-premises storage to the AWS Cloud (File, Volume, and Tape Gateways) for hybrid storage.

questions.prompt: How can a client anticipate future expenses associated with the operation of a new web application?
questions.explanation: To anticipate costs for a new application before deployment, use the AWS Simple Monthly Calculator (now AWS Pricing Calculator). Cost Explorer/CUR work on actual usage; Backtrack is an Aurora feature.

questions.prompt: The term "fault tolerance" relates to the following:

options.text: the ability of an application to accommodate growth without changing design
how well and how quickly an application's environment can have lost data restored
the built-in redundancy of an application's components

questions.explanation: Fault tolerance is the built-in redundancy of an application's components so the workload survives component failure. Growth without redesign is scalability; data restore is recoverability.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service enables conventional SQL queries against stored datasets straight from Amazon S3?
questions.explanation: Amazon Athena lets you run standard SQL queries directly against data stored in S3 — no infrastructure to manage. Glue is ETL, Data Pipeline is workflow, CloudSearch is search.

questions.prompt: A startup is developing a new application that must be launched immediately. In the near future, the application criteria may need to be Which of the following is an AWS Cloud feature that would fulfill this particular requirement?
questions.explanation: Agility — provisioning resources in minutes and easily changing them as requirements evolve — fits a startup that must launch fast and may need to pivot.

questions.prompt: Which characteristics are advantages of using the AWS Cloud? (Choose two.)

options.text: A 100% service level agreement (SLA) for all AWS services
Compute capacity that is adjusted on demand
Availability of AWS Support for code development

questions.explanation: On-demand compute and enhanced security are cloud advantages. AWS doesn't offer a blanket 100% SLA, code dev support isn't a defining cloud benefit, and cost/complexity decrease — not increase.

questions.prompt: A company wants to convert video files and audio files from their source format into a format that will play on smartphones, tablets, and web browsers. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?
questions.explanation: Amazon Elastic Transcoder converts media files between formats suitable for phones, tablets, and browsers. Comprehend is NLP, Glue is ETL, Rekognition is image/video analysis.

questions.prompt: A company has several departments. Each department has its own AWS accounts for its applications. The company wants all AWS costs on a single invoice to simplify payment, but the company wants to know the costs that each department is incurring. Which AWS tool or feature will provide this functionality?
questions.explanation: Consolidated billing (an AWS Organizations feature) gives one invoice across many member accounts while still letting each account's costs be tracked individually.

questions.prompt: A company wants to eliminate the need to guess infrastructure capacity before deployments. The company also wants to spend its budget on cloud resources only as the company uses the resources. Which advantage of the AWS Cloud matches the company's requirements?
questions.explanation: "Stop guessing capacity" plus paying only for resources consumed is the Pay-As-You-Go pricing advantage.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service must be enabled in order for the AWS Management Console to monitor all user account changes?
questions.explanation: AWS CloudTrail records all account API activity including Management Console actions, providing the audit trail of user/account changes. SNS notifies, VPC Flow Logs are network traffic, CloudHSM is key storage.

questions.prompt: Recently, an ecommerce firm began using the AWS Cloud. Which security-related responsibilities fall within the purview of the business? (Select two.)

options.text: Restrict who is allowed physical access to the hosts that run the company's Amazon EC2 instances.
Install security patches on Amazon EC2 Linux instances.
Choose to encrypt data at rest that is stored on Amazon S3.
Wipe Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) volumes clean before they are decommissioned.
Conduct database patching for Amazon RDS instances.

questions.explanation: Installing security patches on EC2 Linux instances and choosing to encrypt data at rest on S3 are customer duties. Physical host access, EBS device wiping, and RDS patching are AWS responsibilities.

questions.prompt: A business needs to guarantee that users of the AWS Management Console adhere to password complexity guidelines. How can a business customize the difficulty of its passwords?

options.text: Using an AWS IAM user policy
Using an AWS Organizations service control policy (SCP)
Using an AWS IAM account password policy
Using an AWS Security Hub managed insight

questions.explanation: The IAM account password policy lets administrators set minimum length, character requirements, rotation, and reuse rules for IAM user passwords. User policies grant permissions, SCPs define org-level limits, Security Hub aggregates findings.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service would determine if a security group has granted unlimited access to a resource?
questions.explanation: AWS Trusted Advisor has a Security check that flags security groups with overly permissive rules (0.0.0.0/0). CloudWatch is metrics, VPC Flow Logs is traffic, CloudTrail is API audit.

questions.prompt: Which features are available to users while using AWS KMS?

options.text: Create and manage AWS access keys for the AWS account root user
Create and manage AWS access keys for an AWS account IAM user
Create and manage keys for encryption and decryption of data
Create and manage keys for multi-factor authentication

questions.explanation: AWS KMS is the managed service for creating, controlling, and auditing encryption keys for data. It does not create IAM access keys or MFA tokens.

questions.prompt: On Amazon EC2 instances, a business has installed various relational databases. Each month, the database software manufacturer publishes new security updates for databases that must be deployed. Which method is the MOST EFFECTIVE for applying security patches?

options.text: Connect to each database instance on a monthly basis, and download and apply the necessary security patches from the vendor.
Enable automatic patching for the instances using the Amazon RDS console.
In AWS Config, configure a rule for the instances and the required patch level.
Use AWS Systems Manager to automate database patching according to a schedule.

questions.explanation: AWS Systems Manager Patch Manager automates patching of self-managed databases on EC2 on a schedule. RDS-console automatic patching only applies to RDS-managed instances; Config tracks configuration; manual SSH per instance does not scale.

questions.prompt: A business is releasing a new application on AWS. The application will be hosted on an Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2) instance. Additional EC2 instances will be required as the demand grows. Which AWS service or technology can the business utilize to deploy the required number of EC2 instances?
questions.explanation: Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling automatically launches and terminates the required number of instances based on demand and policies. ELB distributes traffic but doesn't change capacity.

questions.prompt: What does it mean to provide AWS IAM users the fewest possible privileges?

options.text: It is granting permissions to a single user only.
It is granting permissions using AWS IAM policies only.
It is granting AdministratorAccess policy permissions to trustworthy users.
It is granting only the permissions required to perform a given task.

questions.explanation: Least privilege grants only the permissions required to perform a given task — the minimum needed and no more.

questions.prompt: When building an Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS) instance in Multiple Availability Zone mode, which architectural concept is followed?

options.text: Automate everything that can be automated.

questions.explanation: Building an RDS Multi-AZ instance assumes a single AZ may fail and provides automatic failover to a standby — "design for failure" applied to the database tier.

questions.prompt: What is a user's responsibility while using the AWS Cloud to execute an application?

options.text: Providing a list of users approved for data center access

questions.explanation: Managing application software updates is the customer's responsibility. AWS owns physical hardware, hypervisor, and data center access.

questions.prompt: Which of the following statements concerning AWS's worldwide infrastructure is true?

options.text: Availability Zones can span multiple AWS Regions.
A VPC can have different subnets in different AWS Regions.
AWS Regions consist of multiple Availability Zones.
A single subnet can span multiple Availability Zones.

questions.explanation: AWS Regions consist of multiple Availability Zones. AZs don't span Regions, a VPC is regional (subnets must live in one Region), and a subnet lives in exactly one AZ.

questions.prompt: A business wishes to link AWS to its corporate network through a private network connection. Which Amazon Web Services (AWS) service or functionality will satisfy this requirement?
questions.explanation: AWS Direct Connect provides a private (not internet) network connection between an on-premises corporate network and AWS. VPC peering connects VPCs; Route 53 is DNS; Connect is a contact center.

questions.prompt: Which of the following may be used to restrict certain users’ access to Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) buckets?

options.text: AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) policies

questions.explanation: IAM policies grant or restrict user/role access to S3 buckets and other resources. Key pairs are for SSH, Inspector scans vulnerabilities, security groups are network-level firewalls.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an AWS Well-Architected Framework design principle?

options.text: Reduce downtime by making infrastructure changes infrequently and in large increments.
Invest the time to configure infrastructure manually.
Learn to improve from operational failures.
Use monolithic application design for centralization.

questions.explanation: "Learn from operational failures" (and continuously improve) is a stated Well-Architected design principle. Large infrequent changes, manual configuration, and monolithic design are anti-patterns.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service enables a business to identify and reroute customers to other servers in the event of a website server outage?
questions.explanation: Amazon Route 53 health checks detect endpoint failures and reroute DNS to healthy targets, providing failover for a website server outage.

questions.prompt: An organization with an AWS Support plan for developers established an Amazon RDS database but is unable to connect to it. To get this degree of help, who should the developer contact?

options.text: AWS Support using a support case

questions.explanation: With the Developer Support plan, the developer opens a support case in the AWS Support Center. Professional Services is paid consulting; TAMs and Consulting Partners are not included in Developer Support.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service enables you to monitor and debug distributed applications end-to-end?
questions.explanation: AWS X-Ray traces requests as they travel through a distributed application, surfacing latency, errors, and dependencies end-to-end. Cloud9 is an IDE, CodeStar is project setup, Cloud Map is service discovery.

questions.prompt: Which AWS shared responsibility model duties are the customer’s responsibility? (Select two.)
questions.explanation: Customers configure their own applications and security groups. Facilities access, hardware lifecycle, and network infrastructure protection are AWS responsibilities.

questions.prompt: Which AWS Cloud service gives tips on how to optimize an AWS account’s performance?
questions.explanation: AWS Trusted Advisor's Performance category surfaces optimization tips (idle resources, oversized instances, S3 best practices). Inspector finds vulnerabilities; CloudWatch is metrics; CloudTrail is API logs.

questions.prompt: Which variables impact AWS Cloud costs? (Select two.)

options.text: The number of unused AWS Lambda functions
The number of configured Amazon S3 buckets
Compute resources that are currently in use

questions.explanation: Outbound data transfers (egress) and compute in active use both incur cost. Unused Lambda functions and S3 buckets themselves are free; inbound data transfer to AWS is free.

questions.prompt: A corporation is required by law to track and assess configuration changes to AWS resources, as well as to conduct corrective steps. Which Amazon Web Services (AWS) service should the business use?
questions.explanation: AWS Config tracks resource configuration changes over time and can invoke automated remediation when resources drift out of compliance with rules.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service should be utilized to store data backups for an extended period of time at a reasonable cost?
questions.explanation: Amazon Glacier (now S3 Glacier storage classes) is the lowest-cost option for long-term archival backups. RDS is a DB; Snowball is bulk data transfer; EBS is high-cost block storage.

questions.prompt: What does the AWS Cloud bring clients in terms of increased execution speed and agility? (Select two.)

options.text: Readily available resources with low provisioning times

questions.explanation: Readily available resources with minute-scale provisioning and scalable compute capacity are the textbook speed-and-agility benefits. Free Tier, physical access, and lower cost address different dimensions.

questions.prompt: A retailer wishes to supply just the resources required to meet current demand. Which cloud advantage is the organization attempting to accomplish with this objective?
questions.explanation: Scalability — matching provisioned capacity to current demand — is the cloud advantage being described. Reliability is about uptime; global reach is geographic; HA is fault tolerance.

questions.prompt: What storage capabilities does Amazon S3 Intelligent-Tiering provide?

options.text: Payment flexibility by reserving storage capacity
Long-term retention of data by copying the data to an encrypted Amazon EBS volume
Automatic cost savings by moving objects between tiers based on access pattern changes
Secure, durable, and lowest-cost storage for data archival

questions.explanation: S3 Intelligent-Tiering automatically moves objects between access tiers based on observed access patterns, saving cost on infrequently accessed data without retrieval fees.

questions.prompt: A business wishes to transfer its apps to an AWS VPC. These apps will need access to resources located on-premises. Which combination of activities will allow the business to achieve this objective? (Select two.)

options.text: Use the AWS Service Catalog to identify on-premises resources that can be migrated
Build a VPN connection between an on-premises device and a virtual private gateway in the new VPC
Use Amazon Athena to query data from on-premises database servers
Connect the company’s on-premises data center to AWS using AWS Direct Connect
Leverage Amazon CloudFront to restrict access to static web content hosted on-premises

questions.explanation: A Site-to-Site VPN (over the internet) and AWS Direct Connect (private circuit) are the two standard ways to connect a VPC to on-premises resources. The other options don't establish hybrid network connectivity.

questions.prompt: What is the most effective approach to link an on-premises network to numerous VPCs located in separate AWS Regions?
questions.explanation: AWS Transit Gateway is the hub that interconnects an on-premises network to many VPCs across many Regions through a single point of management.

questions.prompt: Multiple Regions of the AWS Cloud are an example of:
questions.explanation: Multiple AWS Regions is a defining feature of AWS Global Infrastructure. Agility, elasticity, and pay-as-you-go are unrelated benefit names.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service does Chef and Puppet utilize to automate configuration management?
questions.explanation: AWS OpsWorks provides managed instances of Chef and Puppet for configuration management. CloudFormation is IaC, Config is tracking, Systems Manager is operations.

questions.prompt: A corporation wants to remove the necessity for pre-deployment estimation of infrastructure capacity. Additionally, the corporation wants to spend its money on cloud resources only when the resources are used. Which AWS Cloud feature best meets the business’s needs?
questions.explanation: "Stop guessing capacity" plus paying only when resources are used is the Pay-As-You-Go pricing model.

questions.prompt: SQL injection attacks are being launched against an application from a variety of external locations. Which AWS service or functionality can assist in automating response to these attacks?
questions.explanation: AWS WAF lets you define rules (including managed rule groups) to filter and automatically block SQL injection and other Layer-7 attacks. Security groups, ELB, and NACLs operate at L3/L4 and don't inspect HTTP payloads.

questions.prompt: Which feature enables Amazon EC2 instances to be more elastic in response to changing workload demand?
questions.explanation: Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling makes EC2 fleets elastic by adding and removing instances based on demand.

questions.prompt: What time-saving benefits can Amazon Rekognition provide?

options.text: Automatic detection of objects appearing in pictures
Ability to resize millions of images automatically
Use of Amazon Mechanical Turk for human-powered object detection jobs

questions.explanation: Amazon Rekognition automatically detects objects, scenes, and faces in images and video — saving the time of doing it manually or with Mechanical Turk.

questions.prompt: A corporation wants to migrate petabytes of data from on-premises sites to the AWS Cloud as rapidly as feasible. Which Amazon Web Services (AWS) service should the business use?
questions.explanation: AWS Snowball physically ships ruggedized appliances to move petabytes of on-prem data into AWS far faster than the internet. Global Accelerator is anycast traffic routing; Transfer Acceleration helps S3 uploads up to TB; Connect is a contact center.

questions.prompt: Where should a business go to locate, test, purchase, and deploy software that works on AWS?
questions.explanation: AWS Marketplace is the online catalog where customers can find, test, buy, and deploy software that runs on AWS. Lumberyard was a game engine, Artifact is compliance reports, CloudSearch is search.

questions.prompt: A business’s user base is worldwide in scope. The organization needs a highly available application with reduced latency for end users. Which AWS architecture approach will meet these criteria the MOST effectively?
questions.explanation: A multi-Region active-active architecture serves users from the closest Region with low latency and survives any one Region failing. Single-Region designs incur higher latency for distant users.

questions.prompt: Which duty is the customer’s responsibility under the AWS shared responsibility model?

options.text: Maintaining the infrastructure needed to run AWS Lambda
Updating the operating system of Amazon DynamoDB instances
Updating the guest operating system on Amazon EC2 instances

questions.explanation: Updating the guest OS on EC2 is the customer's responsibility. AWS manages Lambda infrastructure, DynamoDB internals, and S3 infrastructure.

questions.prompt: How can a user safeguard against AWS service outages in the event of a widespread natural disaster?

options.text: Deploy applications across multiple Availability Zones within an AWS Region
Use a hybrid cloud computing deployment model within the geographic area
Deploy applications across multiple AWS Regions
Store application artifacts using AWS Artifact and replicate them across multiple AWS Regions

questions.explanation: To survive a widespread natural disaster, deploy across multiple AWS Regions. Multi-AZ within one Region won't survive a regional disaster; AWS Artifact is for compliance docs not app artifacts.

questions.prompt: What is an example of a cloud-based application that is decoupled and scalable?

options.text: A mail and log application that runs on a single Amazon EC2 instance
A webpage hosted on Amazon S3 and using AWS Lambda to update an Amazon DynamoDB database
An Application Load Balancer, web server, and database server supporting a monolithic application
A legacy database server running on the maximum instance size supported by its license

questions.explanation: S3 + Lambda + DynamoDB is a classic decoupled, serverless, scalable pattern — each component scales independently. The other options describe single-instance or monolithic designs.

questions.prompt: A client has many AWS accounts, each with its own billing. How can the client benefit from bulk savings while minimizing the effect on AWS resources?

options.text: Create one global AWS account and move all AWS resources to that account
Sign up for three years of Reserved Instance pricing up front
Use the consolidated billing feature from AWS Organizations
Sign up for the AWS Enterprise Support plan to get volume discounts

questions.explanation: Consolidated billing in AWS Organizations aggregates usage across member accounts so volume discounts apply across the whole org — no need to move resources or commit to RIs upfront.

questions.prompt: A business must keep its data near to its core consumers. Which AWS Cloud advantage satisfies this requirement?
questions.explanation: AWS's global footprint (Regions and edge locations worldwide) lets customers keep data and compute close to their consumers, reducing latency and meeting data residency needs.

questions.prompt: Who is responsible for the virtualization layer down to the physical security of the facilities in which AWS services operate under the AWS shared responsibility model?

options.text: It is the sole responsibility of the customer.
It is the sole responsibility of AWS.
It is a shared responsibility between AWS and the customer.
The customer's AWS Support plan tier determines who manages the configuration.

questions.explanation: Everything from the virtualization layer down to physical facility security is solely AWS's responsibility — "security of the cloud."

questions.prompt: A company's managed IAM policy does not allow users the rights essential to do needed activities. How is this situation to be resolved?

options.text: Use a third-party web application firewall (WAF) managed rule from the AWS Marketplace
Use AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) to create a customer-managed key

questions.explanation: When AWS managed policies don't grant required rights, create a custom (customer-managed) IAM policy with the needed permissions. Shield, WAF, and KMS don't grant IAM rights.

questions.prompt: A business wishes to strengthen its security and audit posture by restricting incoming access to Amazon EC2. What should the organization use instead of opening incoming SSH ports and handling SSH keys to remotely access instances?
questions.explanation: AWS Systems Manager Session Manager provides browser-based shell access to EC2 without opening inbound SSH ports or managing SSH keys — far better posture than open SSH.

questions.prompt: Which scenarios warrant the utilization of Amazon EC2 Spot Instances?

options.text: A company wants to move its main website to AWS from an on-premises web server.
A company has a number of application services whose SLA requires 99.999% uptime.
A company's heavily used legacy database is currently running on-premises.
A company has a number of infrequent, interruptible jobs that are currently using On-Demand Instances.

questions.explanation: Spot Instances fit infrequent, interruptible workloads at up to ~90% discount. An always-on website (A), 99.999% SLA workloads (B), and a critical legacy DB (C) shouldn't run on Spot.

questions.prompt: A business must transmit time-sensitive communications to a large number of subscribers using a push technique. Which Amazon Web Services (AWS) service should the business use?
questions.explanation: Amazon SNS is the AWS pub/sub push service for delivering time-sensitive notifications (SMS, email, mobile push, HTTP) to many subscribers. Kinesis is streaming, MQ/SQS are queues (pull).

questions.prompt: Which tasks need access to the root user of the AWS account? (Select two.)

options.text: Modifying an Amazon EC2 instance type
Grouping resources in AWS Systems Manager
Running applications in Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS)

questions.explanation: Changing the AWS Support plan and closing the AWS account both require root user credentials. EC2 instance type changes, Systems Manager resource grouping, and EKS app operations are IAM-permitted tasks.

questions.prompt: Which AWS Cloud best practice makes advantage of cloud computing's flexibility and agility?

options.text: Provision capacity based on past usage and theoretical peaks
Dynamically and predictively scale to meet usage demands
Build the application and infrastructure in a data center that grants physical access
Break apart the application into loosely coupled components

questions.explanation: Dynamically and predictively scaling to meet usage demand is the cloud-native pattern that exploits flexibility and agility. Static provisioning and physical data-center access are anti-patterns.

questions.prompt: Users of Amazon Route 53 are able to:

options.text: establish a dedicated network connection to AWS

questions.explanation: Amazon Route 53 can register DNS domains as well as host authoritative DNS. Encryption in transit is ACM/TLS; SSL certificates are ACM; dedicated connections are Direct Connect.

questions.prompt: Which characteristic of the Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (Amazon VPC) allows customers to link two VPCs?
questions.explanation: VPC peering connects two VPCs (same or different Region) for private routing. VPC endpoints reach AWS services; ClassicLink is deprecated; Direct Connect is for on-prem.

questions.prompt: Multiple Amazon EC2 instances are used to host an application. The program sends messages using Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS). Which AWS service or feature grants authorization for the application to access needed AWS services?
questions.explanation: An IAM role attached to the EC2 instance (via an instance profile) provides temporary credentials so the app can call SNS without embedding access keys. ACM is for TLS, Security Hub aggregates findings, GuardDuty detects threats.

questions.prompt: A business is contemplating migrating to the AWS Cloud. The firm wishes to be able to scale its computing capacity in response to changing demand conditions. Which AWS Cloud advantage does this case illustrate?
questions.explanation: Scaling compute capacity up and down in response to demand is elasticity. Agility is about deployment speed; global deployments concern reach; cost savings is an outcome but not the named advantage.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a NoSQL database service that is both quick and dependable?
questions.explanation: Amazon DynamoDB is AWS's managed NoSQL database delivering single-digit-millisecond performance. Redshift is a data warehouse, RDS is relational, S3 is object storage.

questions.prompt: A client wants to develop and construct a new workload on AWS Cloud but lacks the necessary technical skills in AWS-related technologies. Which of the following AWS programs may a client use to accomplish that goal?
questions.explanation: AWS Partner Network Consulting Partners help customers design and build on AWS when in-house expertise is limited. Technology Partners sell software; Marketplace is a catalog; Service Catalog distributes approved products.

questions.prompt: Which AWS solution enables rapid setup and management of a new multi-account AWS environment?
questions.explanation: AWS Control Tower automates the setup of a secure, multi-account AWS environment (landing zone) with governance guardrails.

questions.prompt: Which operation needs the usage of the root account user credentials for the AWS account?
questions.explanation: Closing an AWS account requires the root user. Creating log files, modifying IAM user permissions, and deleting IAM users can all be done by appropriately permissioned IAM users.

questions.prompt: On Amazon EC2 instances, a business is operating and controlling its own Docker environment. An alternate is desired to assist in managing cluster size, scheduling, and environment management. Which AWS service satisfies these criteria?
questions.explanation: AWS Fargate is the serverless engine for containers — AWS manages cluster size, scheduling, and the environment, so the customer no longer manages Docker hosts themselves.

questions.prompt: Which AWS situations exemplify the idea of elasticity? (Select two.)

options.text: Scaling the number of Amazon EC2 instances based on traffic
Resizing Amazon RDS instances as business needs change
Automatically directing traffic to less-utilized Amazon EC2 instances
Using AWS compliance documents to accelerate the compliance process
Having the ability to create and govern environments using code

questions.explanation: Auto-scaling EC2 by traffic and resizing RDS as needs change are both expressions of elasticity. Traffic routing is load balancing, compliance docs are unrelated, IaC is automation/IaC.

questions.prompt: Which of the following are advantages of using the AWS Cloud to host infrastructure? (Select two.)

options.text: AWS manages all security in the cloud
Users have the ability to provision resources on demand
Users have access to free and unlimited storage
Users have control over the physical infrastructure

questions.explanation: No upfront commitments and on-demand resource provisioning are stated cloud advantages. AWS doesn't manage all security (shared model), there's no free unlimited storage, and customers don't get physical infrastructure control.

questions.prompt: Which of the following are benefits of Amazon Web Services' cloud computing platform? (Select two.)

options.text: AWS manages the maintenance of the cloud infrastructure
AWS manages the security of applications built on AWS
AWS manages capacity planning for physical servers
AWS manages the development of applications on AWS
AWS manages cost planning for virtual servers

questions.explanation: AWS manages cloud-infrastructure maintenance and capacity planning for physical servers. Application security and development remain customer responsibilities; cost planning is the customer's decision.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service enables infrastructure as code management?
questions.explanation: AWS CloudFormation defines infrastructure as code (declarative YAML/JSON templates). CodePipeline is CI/CD orchestration, CodeDeploy applies code releases, Direct Connect is networking.

questions.prompt: How might AWS help a business manage costs when an application's consumption varies unpredictably?

options.text: AWS will refund the cost difference if a customer moves to larger servers
The application can be built to scale up or down automatically as resources are needed
Spot instances will automatically be used if the price is lower than on-demand instances
Amazon CloudWatch will automatically predict what resources are needed

questions.explanation: Building the application to scale automatically (up for demand, down to save cost) is how AWS helps manage costs under unpredictable usage. AWS doesn't refund on resize; Spot isn't automatic for everything; CloudWatch doesn't predict capacity by itself.

questions.prompt: What are the benefits of deploying an application across various Availability Zones using Amazon EC2 instances? (Select two.)

options.text: Preventing a single point of failure
Reducing the operational costs of the application
Allowing the application to serve cross-region users with low latency
Increasing the availability of the application
Increasing the load of the application

questions.explanation: Multi-AZ EC2 deployments prevent a single point of failure and increase availability. Cost typically rises slightly (more instances), cross-Region latency isn't addressed, and load is not increased — it's spread.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service supports a hybrid architecture that gives users the ability to extend AWS infrastructure, AWS services, APIs, and tools to data centers, co-location environments, or on-premises facilities?
questions.explanation: AWS Outposts extends AWS infrastructure, services, APIs, and tools to on-premises facilities for a true hybrid experience. Snowmobile is data transfer, Local Zones extend metros, Fargate is serverless containers.

questions.prompt: A company has a physical tape library to store data backups. The tape library is running out of space. The company needs to extend the tape library's capacity to the AWS Cloud. Which AWS service should the company use to meet this requirement?
questions.explanation: AWS Storage Gateway's Tape Gateway integrates with existing tape libraries and backs the data to S3/Glacier transparently. EBS, S3, and EFS don't speak the iSCSI VTL protocol.

questions.prompt: A business wishes to downsize its infrastructure in order to save money. At what stages should a business downsize? (Select two.)

options.text: Rightsize before a migration occurs to the cloud
Rightsize continuously after the cloud onboarding process
Rightsize when AWS Support calls and explains that rightsizing is needed
Rightsize when seasonal workloads are at their peak
Rightsize after purchasing all Reserved Instances

questions.explanation: Rightsize before migration (so you start cloud usage efficient) and continuously after onboarding (workloads change). Rightsizing only at peaks or after RI purchases is too late and locks in waste.

questions.prompt: AWS Budgets may be used for the following purposes:

options.text: prevent a given user from creating a resource
send an alert when the utilization of Reserved Instances drops below a certain percentage
set resource limits in AWS accounts to prevent overspending
split an AWS bill across multiple forms of payment

questions.explanation: AWS Budgets supports RI/Savings-Plans utilization alerts — you can be notified when RI utilization drops below a target. Budgets alerts but doesn't prevent resource creation or split bills across payment methods.

questions.prompt: An online retail company has seasonal sales spikes several times a year, primarily around holidays. Demand is lower at other times. The company finds it difficult to predict the increasing infrastructure demand for each season. Which advantages of moving to the AWS Cloud would MOST benefit the company? (Choose two.)
questions.explanation: Elasticity (scale for seasonal peaks, down at quiet times) and pay-as-you-go pricing (no fixed commitment) directly address unpredictable seasonal demand.

questions.prompt: What are the advantages of unified billing for Amazon Web Services accounts?

options.text: Access to AWS Personal Health Dashboard

questions.explanation: Consolidated billing produces combined-usage volume discounts across all member accounts (S3, data transfer, etc.). It doesn't change Personal Health Dashboard access, security posture, or IAM.

questions.prompt: A corporation wants to connect to AWS from a distant office through a private, low-latency connection. Which strategy is advised for meeting these requirements?

options.text: Use VPC peering to create a connection

questions.explanation: AWS Direct Connect provides a private, low-latency, dedicated network link from an on-prem location to AWS. Internet VPN has higher jitter; VPC peering is VPC-to-VPC.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service can be used to turn text into lifelike speech?
questions.explanation: Amazon Polly converts text to lifelike speech in dozens of voices and languages. Kendra is enterprise search, Rekognition is image/video, Connect is contact center.

questions.prompt: Which Amazon EC2 pricing model is dynamically adjusted in response to EC2 instance availability and demand?
questions.explanation: Spot Instance pricing fluctuates with EC2 supply and demand — customers pay the current Spot price (subject to a cap). On-Demand and Reserved pricing are static.

questions.prompt: Which service should a client utilize to integrate and manage several Amazon Web Services accounts centrally?
questions.explanation: AWS Organizations centrally manages and integrates multiple AWS accounts with consolidated billing, SCPs, and account governance. IAM handles users within one account, SCT is for DB migration, Config is for configuration tracking.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service or tool can be used to capture information about inbound and outbound traffic in an Amazon VPC?
questions.explanation: VPC Flow Logs capture metadata about inbound and outbound IP traffic in a VPC and publish it to CloudWatch Logs or S3. Inspector scans for vulnerabilities; endpoints are network access; NAT gateways are SNAT.

questions.prompt: Which choices do users have when contacting AWS Support?

options.text: Create an email case in the AWS Support Center.
Visit a local AWS Support Center.
Call the customer service phone number.
Use the video conference functionality of the AWS Support console.

questions.explanation: Email/web cases via the AWS Support Center are available on every plan. Live chat and phone are reserved for Business+ plans; there is no local AWS Support office or video conference for support.

questions.prompt: A business must track and anticipate AWS expenditures and use. Additionally, the organization must configure event-driven alerts that are triggered when expenditure limitations are exceeded. Which AWS offering or technology should the business employ?
questions.explanation: AWS Budgets lets you set custom spend or usage thresholds and triggers email/SNS alerts on actual or forecasted overage.

questions.prompt: Which of the following IT functions does AWS do in order to relieve a business of its responsibility for managing its IT resources? (Select two.)

options.text: Setting up access controls for data

questions.explanation: For managed services, AWS handles database backups and installing/maintaining the operating system. Customers still configure OS firewalls (on EC2), data access controls, and database user accounts.

questions.prompt: Which AWS technologies aid in cost estimation? (Select three.)

options.text: AWS Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) Calculator

questions.explanation: Cost-estimation tools include the Detailed Billing Report (historical), AWS Simple Monthly Calculator / Pricing Calculator (pre-deployment), and the TCO Calculator (vs. on-prem). Cost allocation tags slice existing spend but don't estimate; there is no service called "Cost Estimator."

questions.prompt: A company wants to ensure that two Amazon EC2 instances are in separate data centers with minimal communication latency between the data centers. How can the company meet this requirement?

options.text: Place the EC2 instances in two separate AWS Regions connected with a VPC peering connection.
Place the EC2 instances in two separate Availability Zones within the same AWS Region.
Place one EC2 instance on premises and the other in an AWS Region. Then connect them by using an AWS VPN connection.
Place both EC2 instances in a placement group for dedicated bandwidth.

questions.explanation: Two AZs in the same Region put the instances in separate data centers with low-latency private connectivity between them. Cross-Region adds latency; on-prem + AWS is hybrid; placement groups affect networking but not data-center separation.

questions.prompt: In comparison to conventional and virtualized data center prices, AWS offers the following:

options.text: greater variable costs and greater upfront costs.
fixed usage costs and lower upfront costs.
lower variable costs and greater upfront costs.
lower variable costs and lower upfront costs.

questions.explanation: AWS replaces large upfront capital expenses with lower variable operating expenses — both lower variable and lower upfront.

questions.prompt: Which situations should the AWS Abuse team be notified about?

options.text: An Availability Zone has a service disruption
An intrusion attempt is made from an AWS IP address
A user has trouble accessing an Amazon S3 bucket from an AWS IP address
A user needs to change payment methods due to a compromise

questions.explanation: Intrusion attempts originating from AWS IP addresses should be reported to the AWS Abuse team. AZ disruptions are surfaced via Service Health Dashboard; access trouble and payment changes go to Support.

questions.prompt: Which AWS feature should a client exploit to ensure an application's high availability?
questions.explanation: Distributing across Availability Zones is the AWS feature that delivers high availability — survives any single AZ failure. Direct Connect is connectivity, data centers aren't customer-controlled, VPC is a network boundary.

questions.prompt: In which situations should a company create an IAM user instead of an IAM role? (Select two.)

options.text: When an application that runs on Amazon EC2 instances requires access to other AWS services
When the company creates AWS access credentials for individuals
When the company creates an application that runs on a mobile phone that makes requests to AWS
When the company needs to add users to IAM groups
When users are authenticated in the corporate network and want to use AWS without signing in a second time

questions.explanation: Use IAM users for individuals who need long-lived credentials and to add to IAM groups for permissions inheritance. Apps on EC2 should use IAM roles; mobile apps should use Cognito or role temporary credentials; SSO is handled by IAM Identity Center.

questions.prompt: How might an AWS client implement common access restrictions to a big group of users easily?

options.text: Apply an IAM policy to an IAM group.
Apply an IAM policy to an IAM role.
Apply the same IAM policy to all IAM users with access to the same workload.
Apply an IAM policy to an Amazon Cognito user pool.

questions.explanation: Attach an IAM policy to an IAM group and add the users — every group member inherits the permissions. Policies on individual users don't scale; Cognito user pools are app-level identity.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service is used to provide encryption for Amazon EBS?
questions.explanation: AWS KMS provides the encryption keys used to encrypt EBS volumes. ACM is for TLS certificates, Systems Manager is operations, Config is configuration tracking.

questions.prompt: A business needs software solutions that are either hosted on the AWS platform or are linked with it. Independent software providers, as well as management and security vendors, are required to provide solutions. Which organization or team is capable of providing these solutions?
questions.explanation: AWS Partner Network Technology Partners are ISVs and management/security vendors whose products run on or integrate with AWS. TAMs, Consulting Partners, and Concierge are service offerings, not solution providers.

questions.prompt: Which AWS services are available for application deployment? (Select two.)
questions.explanation: AWS Elastic Beanstalk and AWS OpsWorks are application-deployment services. Config is configuration tracking, Application Discovery Service is for migration assessment, Kinesis is streaming.

questions.prompt: Which AWS services make use of global edge locations? (Select two.)
questions.explanation: Amazon CloudFront (CDN) and AWS Global Accelerator both leverage AWS's global edge-location network. Fargate is serverless containers, Wavelength is 5G edge, VPC is a virtual network.

questions.prompt: Which responsibility do customers bear while using Amazon EC2? (Select two.)

options.text: Physical security at data center facilities

questions.explanation: With EC2 the customer manages file-system encryption and guest OS firewall (iptables, Windows firewall) configuration. Hardware, hypervisor patching, and physical security are AWS responsibilities.

questions.prompt: A company is operating several factories where it builds products. The company needs the ability to process data, store data, and run applications with local system interdependencies that require low latency. Which AWS service should the company use to meet these requirements?
questions.explanation: AWS Outposts brings AWS compute, storage, and APIs on-prem to factories, supporting low-latency local apps with system interdependencies. Greengrass is for IoT device fleets; Snowball Edge is for transient compute/storage.

questions.prompt: A business needs to handle a huge volume of data from social network accounts using high-throughput graphical queries. Which AWS service will assist the business in developing a cloud architecture that satisfies these criteria?
questions.explanation: Amazon Neptune is the managed graph database, ideal for social-network style data and graph queries. RDS/Aurora are relational, DynamoDB is key-value, Redshift is analytical.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service enables the use of the AWS Cloud to host a NoSQL database?
questions.explanation: Amazon DynamoDB is AWS's managed NoSQL key-value/document database. Aurora and RDS are relational; Redshift is a data warehouse.

questions.prompt: Which of the following acts as an instance-level firewall to control inbound and outbound access?
questions.explanation: Security groups are stateful, instance-level firewalls. Network ACLs operate at the subnet level (stateless); Trusted Advisor is checks; virtual private gateways connect VPN.

questions.prompt: A business is consolidating many apps into a single AWS account. The organization wishes to keep track of the AWS Cloud fees paid by individual applications. What can the business do to ensure compliance with this requirement?

options.text: Set the budgets in Cost Explorer.

questions.explanation: Cost allocation tags applied to resources let you break out spend by application in Cost Explorer and the CUR — even when everything lives in a single AWS account.

questions.prompt: On an Amazon EC2 instance, a batch job takes 5 hours to complete. Monthly, the quantity of data to be processed doubles, and the time required to process it is proportionate. What is the optimal cloud architecture for meeting this escalating demand?

options.text: Run the application on a bigger EC2 instance size.
Switch to an EC2 instance family that better matches batch requirements.
Distribute the application across multiple EC2 instances and run the workload in parallel.
Run the application on a bare metal EC2 instance.

questions.explanation: To handle doubling data each month within a fixed wall-clock window, distribute the workload across multiple EC2 instances and run in parallel (horizontal scaling). A bigger or different instance type alone has limits; bare metal doesn't change the parallelism story.

questions.prompt: A company has a workload that will run continuously for 1 year. The workload cannot tolerate service interruptions. Which Amazon EC2 purchasing option will be MOST cost-effective?
questions.explanation: All Upfront Reserved Instances give the deepest discount for a workload that runs continuously for a year and cannot tolerate interruption. Partial Upfront is next; Dedicated Instances are pricier; On-Demand is full price.

questions.prompt: How can AWS Trusted Advisor assist AWS Cloud users? (Select two.)

options.text: It identifies software vulnerabilities in applications running on AWS
