It provides a list of cost optimization recommendations based on current AWS usage
It detects potential security vulnerabilities caused by permissions settings on account resources
It automatically corrects potential security issues caused by permissions settings on account resources
It provides proactive alerting whenever an Amazon EC2 instance has been compromised

questions.explanation: Trusted Advisor provides cost-optimization recommendations and flags security findings caused by permissions. It does not scan applications for software vulnerabilities (Inspector does), apply auto-remediation, or alert on instance compromise (GuardDuty does).

questions.prompt: Which AWS function will assist users in determining the CPU capacity of an application running on an Amazon EC2 instance?
questions.explanation: Amazon CloudWatch collects CPU utilization metrics for EC2 instances. Config tracks configuration; CloudTrail tracks API calls; Inspector finds vulnerabilities.

questions.prompt: A user may create a master payer account in order to access aggregated billing data via:
questions.explanation: AWS Organizations lets a master payer account aggregate billing from member accounts.

questions.prompt: Every few years, a business invests several months in modernizing its on-premises infrastructure. The organization wishes to shorten the time required for infrastructure purchase by shifting to the AWS Cloud. What is the primary advantage of transferring this use case to the AWS Cloud?

options.text: AWS will help move the existing hardware to the AWS data centers.
The company will have increased agility with on-demand access to IT resources.
Enterprise support will be available to help with recurring application installation and setup.
The company will experience less downtime with Multi-AZ deployments.

questions.explanation: Moving to AWS replaces multi-month procurement cycles with on-demand access to IT resources — the agility advantage of the cloud.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service helps protect against DDoS attacks?
questions.explanation: AWS Shield (Standard at no extra cost; Advanced as a paid tier) protects AWS resources against DDoS attacks. Inspector scans for vulnerabilities, GuardDuty detects threats, Detective investigates findings.

questions.prompt: Which Amazon Web Services (AWS) offering allows customers to aggregate billing for many accounts?
questions.explanation: AWS Organizations aggregates billing across many AWS accounts (consolidated billing). QuickSight is BI, Budgets sets spend alerts, Forecast is ML forecasting.

questions.prompt: Which of the following are advantages of Amazon Web Services' cloud computing platform? (Select two.)
questions.explanation: Elasticity (scale with demand) and Agility (provision in minutes) are stated cloud advantages. No service has "unlimited uptime," colocation is on-prem, and AWS replaces capex with opex.

questions.prompt: An application installed in the AWS Cloud exhibits irregular consumption patterns and is responsible for non-stop workloads. Which Amazon EC2 pricing plan is the MOST cost-effective for this application?
questions.explanation: For non-stop workloads (steady-state always running), Reserved Instances deliver the deepest discount over On-Demand. Spot can be reclaimed; Dedicated is pricier; On-Demand has no commitment.

questions.prompt: Which service's primary aim is to manage software versions?
questions.explanation: AWS CodeCommit is the managed Git source control service whose primary purpose is software version control. CodeStar is project setup, the CLI is a tool, Cognito is identity.

questions.prompt: A business is now undergoing a security examination. The audit includes a security and compliance review of the company's AWS infrastructure and services. The auditor must identify and download AWS security and compliance materials. The System and Organization Control (SOC) reports are one of these documents. Which AWS service or collection of services is capable of providing these documents?
questions.explanation: AWS Artifact is the on-demand self-service portal for compliance reports (SOC, PCI, ISO, FedRAMP). The Abuse team handles security incidents; Support handles tickets; Config tracks configuration.

questions.prompt: Using AWS Config to record, audit, and evaluate changes to AWS resources to enable traceability is an example of which AWS Well-Architected Framework pillar?
questions.explanation: "Enable traceability" — monitor, alert, and audit changes in real time — is a Security pillar design principle. AWS Config recording configuration history maps to this principle.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a design concept for AWS Cloud architecture?
questions.explanation: Loose coupling is a stated AWS Cloud design principle. Single points of failure, monolithic design, and exclusive vertical scaling are anti-patterns.

questions.prompt: The continuous lowering in AWS Cloud price is a result of the following:
questions.explanation: AWS's massive economies of scale — aggregated demand across many customers — drive the continuous reductions in cloud pricing.

questions.prompt: Which phase discusses agility as an advantage of AWS Cloud-based development?

options.text: The ability to pay only when computing resources are consumed, based on the volume of resources that are consumed
The ability to eliminate guessing about infrastructure capacity needs
The ability to support innovation through a reduction in the time that is required to make IT resources available to developers
The ability to deploy an application in multiple AWS Regions around the world in minutes

questions.explanation: Agility is the cloud's ability to reduce the time required to make IT resources available to developers — supporting faster innovation. The other options describe pay-as-you-go, capacity planning, and global reach.

questions.prompt: Which AWS tool or feature acts as a VPC firewall at the subnet level?
questions.explanation: Network ACLs operate at the subnet level inside a VPC as stateless firewalls. Security groups are at the instance level. Traffic Mirroring captures packets; IGW provides internet egress.

questions.prompt: A business wishes to deliver managed Windows virtual desktops and programs through secure network connections to its distant workers. Which AWS services does the business have access to in order to achieve these requirements? (Select two.)
questions.explanation: Amazon WorkSpaces provides managed Windows virtual desktops; AWS Site-to-Site VPN provides secure network connections from remote workers/sites. AppStream 2.0 streams apps not desktops; Connect is contact center; ECS is containers.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an AWS shared responsibility?
questions.explanation: IAM is a shared responsibility: AWS provides the service and infrastructure, the customer configures users, groups, roles, and policies. SSE setup is customer; firewall configuration is customer; physical hardware is AWS-only.

questions.prompt: Which storage service can be utilized to host static webpages at a minimal cost?
questions.explanation: Amazon S3 static website hosting serves HTML/CSS/JS at very low cost. Glacier is for archives, DynamoDB is a database, EFS is shared file storage.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service can be used to decouple applications?
questions.explanation: Amazon SQS message queues let producers and consumers run independently — the canonical decoupling primitive. Config is configuration tracking, Batch runs batch jobs, SES is email.

questions.prompt: Which AWS services are globally specified rather than regionally defined? (Select two.)
questions.explanation: Amazon Route 53 (DNS) and Amazon CloudFront (CDN) are global services. EC2, S3, and DynamoDB are regional services (S3 buckets and DynamoDB tables live in a Region).

questions.prompt: Currently, a company's online program is highly dependent on underlying components, which means that when one component fails, the whole website crashes. Which AWS Cloud design concept is most appropriate for resolving the present design issue?

options.text: Implementing elasticity, enabling the application to scale up or scale down as demand changes.
Enabling several EC2 instances to run in parallel to achieve better performance.
Focusing on decoupling components by isolating them and ensuring individual components can function when other components fail.
Doubling EC2 computing resources to increase system fault tolerance.

questions.explanation: Decoupling components — isolating them so one failure doesn't cascade — directly addresses the described tightly-coupled-failure problem. Elasticity and parallelism don't fix coupling; doubling resources can't survive coupled-component failure.

questions.prompt: A user must locate, categorize, and safeguard sensitive data stored in Amazon S3 automatically. Which AWS service satisfies these criteria?
questions.explanation: Amazon Macie uses ML to automatically discover, classify, and protect sensitive data (PII, PHI, financial) stored in Amazon S3. Inspector is vulnerability scanning; GuardDuty is threat detection; Secrets Manager stores secrets.

questions.prompt: On an Amazon EC2 instance, a business hosts an application. Several AWS resources, including Amazon S3 and Amazon DynamoDB, are required by the EC2 instance. What is the MOST OPTIMAL way to delegate permissions?

options.text: Create an IAM role with the required permissions. Attach the role to the EC2 instance.
Create an IAM user and use its access key and secret access key in the application.
Create an IAM user and use its access key and secret access key to create a CLI profile in the EC2 instance.
Create an IAM role with the required permissions. Attach the role to the administrative IAM user.

questions.explanation: Create an IAM role with the required permissions and attach it to the EC2 instance via an instance profile — temporary, automatically rotated credentials, no embedded keys. Hard-coded user keys are an anti-pattern.

questions.prompt: Which of the following are support categories for AWS Trusted Advisor? (Select two.)
questions.explanation: Trusted Advisor's 5 categories: Cost Optimization, Performance, Security, Fault Tolerance, Service Limits. Of the options, Cost optimization and Security match.

questions.prompt: Which disaster recovery option is the LEAST expensive?
questions.explanation: Backup and restore is the least expensive of the AWS DR strategies — store backups only; restore on demand. Pilot light, Warm standby, and Multi-site cost progressively more.

questions.prompt: Which type of AWS storage is ephemeral and is deleted when an Amazon EC2 instance is stopped or terminated?
questions.explanation: Amazon EC2 Instance Store is ephemeral — data is lost when the instance stops or terminates. EBS, EFS, and S3 all persist independently of instance lifecycle.

questions.prompt: A company needs fully managed, highly reliable, and scalable file storage that is accessible over the Server Message Block (SMB) protocol. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?

options.text: Amazon FSx for Windows File Server

questions.explanation: Amazon FSx for Windows File Server provides fully managed Windows file storage accessible via the SMB protocol with Active Directory integration. EFS is NFS-only; S3 is object; EBS is block.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a component of the AWS Global Infrastructure?
questions.explanation: AWS Regions are core components of the AWS Global Infrastructure (along with Availability Zones and edge locations). Alexa, Lightsail, and Organizations aren't infrastructure components.

questions.prompt: What is the purpose of having an internet gateway within a VPC?

options.text: To create a VPN connection to the VPC
To allow communication between the VPC and the internet
To impose bandwidth constraints on internet traffic
To load balance traffic from the internet across Amazon EC2 Instances

questions.explanation: An internet gateway allows the VPC to communicate with the public internet (in and out). It does not throttle bandwidth, terminate VPNs, or load balance.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service allows users to download security and compliance reports about the AWS infrastructure on demand?
questions.explanation: AWS Artifact provides on-demand downloads of AWS security and compliance reports (SOC, ISO, PCI, FedRAMP).

questions.prompt: A company is planning an infrastructure deployment to the AWS Cloud. Before the deployment, the company wants a cost estimate for running the infrastructure. Which AWS service or feature can provide this information?
questions.explanation: AWS Pricing Calculator estimates costs before deployment. Cost Explorer / CUR analyze historical spend; Trusted Advisor checks best practices.

questions.prompt: How can Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling groups contribute to a web application's high availability?

options.text: They automatically add more instances across multiple AWS Regions based on global demand of the application.
They automatically add or replace instances across multiple Availability Zones when the application needs it.
They enable the application's static content to reside closer to end users.
They are able to distribute incoming requests across a tier of web server instances.

questions.explanation: EC2 Auto Scaling groups automatically add or replace instances across multiple Availability Zones to maintain capacity and recover from instance/AZ failures, increasing HA.

questions.prompt: A business has a stateless application workload that can withstand brief periods of outage. The program conducts calculations in massively parallel fashion. Which Amazon EC2 pricing model should the business choose to save costs?
questions.explanation: Stateless, interruptible, massively parallel workloads are the textbook fit for Spot Instances (up to ~90% off On-Demand).

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a method for enhancing AWS security? (Select two.)

options.text: Granting the broadest permissions to all IAM roles
Running application code with AWS Cloud9
Enabling multi-factor authentication (MFA) with Amazon Cognito
Using AWS Trusted Advisor security checks

questions.explanation: MFA on accounts/users and Trusted Advisor security checks both materially improve AWS security posture. Artifact is documentation; broad permissions weaken security; Cloud9 is an IDE.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service or tool helps to centrally manage billing and allow controlled access to resources across AWS accounts?
questions.explanation: AWS Organizations centrally manages billing and applies controlled-access governance (SCPs, OUs) across multiple AWS accounts. IAM handles users within one account; Cost Explorer/Budgets are billing analytics.

questions.prompt: Under the AWS shared responsibility paradigm, the following parties are responsible for the security and patching of the guest operating system:
questions.explanation: Patching and securing the guest operating system on EC2 is the customer's responsibility under the shared model.

questions.prompt: What is an example of AWS Cloud's high availability?

options.text: Consulting AWS technical support at any time day or night
Ensuring an application remains accessible, even if a resource fails
Making any AWS service available for use by paying on demand
Deploying in any part of the world using AWS Regions

questions.explanation: High availability means the application remains accessible even if an underlying resource fails. The other options describe support, pricing, and global reach.

questions.prompt: Which of the following are Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (Amazon VPC) resources?
questions.explanation: Subnets and internet gateways are VPC resources. Objects/ACLs and buckets are S3, groups/roles are IAM, access policies span multiple services.

questions.prompt: Which aspects of AWS make it cost viable for workloads with fluctuating customer demand? (Select two.)
questions.explanation: Elasticity (scale to match demand) and Pay-as-you-go pricing (only pay for what you use) make AWS cost-viable for fluctuating workloads.

questions.prompt: A business wants to migrate 40 B of data from on-premises storage systems to Amazon S3. The transfer must occur as rapidly as feasible while maintaining a low cost. The firm has internet access at a speed of 100 megabits per second. Which AWS service will satisfy these criteria?
questions.explanation: Over 100 Mbps internet, 40 TB would take roughly a month to transfer. AWS Snowball ships physical devices and delivers in days — faster and cheaper for large bulk migrations.

questions.prompt: Which of the following allows customers to programmatically access the power of AWS services?
questions.explanation: The AWS CLI provides programmatic access to AWS services from the command line. Trusted Advisor is checks; CodeDeploy is deployment; the Management Console is a UI.

questions.prompt: A business wishes to migrate some of its resources to the AWS Cloud. To comply with regulatory standards, data must stay on-premises and local. AWS and the company's resources must have a low latency. Which Amazon Web Services (AWS) service or functionality may be leveraged to fulfill these requirements?
questions.explanation: AWS Outposts brings AWS infrastructure on-premises so data can remain local (regulatory) while still using AWS services and APIs with low latency. Local Zones are AWS-managed near metros, not on-prem; Availability Zones aren't customer hostable.

questions.prompt: A business has specified the Amazon Web Services (AWS) resources it requires for a new application. The business must determine the expenses associated with operating the application on AWS. What actions should the business take to comply with this stipulation?

options.text: Take advantage of AWS on-demand pricing.
Use the AWS Pricing Calculator to generate an approximate dollar amount.
Use Amazon QuickSight to analyze current on-premises spending.
Use Amazon AppStream 2.0 for real-time pricing analytics.

questions.explanation: AWS Pricing Calculator generates approximate dollar-amount cost estimates for proposed AWS architectures before deployment.

questions.prompt: For its steady-state database, a corporation runs a self-managed Oracle database directly on Amazon EC2. The corporation wishes to cut its computing expenses. Which option should the business choose in order to optimize savings over a three-year period?
questions.explanation: For a steady-state self-managed Oracle DB on EC2 over 3 years, Reserved Instances (especially All-Upfront) maximize savings vs On-Demand. Spot can be reclaimed; Dedicated Instances are pricier.

questions.prompt: A corporation is developing an application that must distribute photos and videos internationally with the least amount of delay possible. Which strategy may the business take to do this in the most cost-effective manner?

options.text: Deliver the content through Amazon CloudFront.
Store the content on Amazon S3 and enable S3 cross-region replication.
Implement a VPN across multiple AWS Regions.
Deliver the content through AWS PrivateLink.

questions.explanation: Amazon CloudFront's global edge network is the most cost-effective way to distribute photos and videos worldwide with low latency. S3 cross-region replication moves data but doesn't deliver at low latency; VPN/PrivateLink are wrong tools.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service or functionality gives advice to customers about frequently asked billing questions?
questions.explanation: The AWS Knowledge Center publishes answers to frequently asked billing and account questions. Marketplace sells products, Pinpoint is messaging, Connect is contact center.

questions.prompt: Which AWS services enable an on-premises infrastructure to be extended to the AWS Cloud? (Select two.)
questions.explanation: AWS Direct Connect (private network link) and AWS Storage Gateway (hybrid storage) extend on-premises infrastructure into the AWS Cloud. EBS, CloudFront, and Connect don't serve this purpose.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service enables the generation of warnings based on a monthly bill estimate?
questions.explanation: Amazon CloudWatch supports billing alarms — triggered when estimated charges exceed a threshold. Config, X-Ray, and CloudTrail don't generate billing-based warnings.

questions.prompt: A company needs to identify the last time that a specific user accessed the AWS Management Console. Which AWS service will provide this information?
questions.explanation: AWS CloudTrail records who signed in to the Management Console and when — including the last-access timestamp. Cognito is app user identity; Inspector scans vulnerabilities; GuardDuty detects threats.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service may be utilized to deploy the application?
questions.explanation: AWS CodePipeline is a CI/CD service that automates the build, test, and deploy stages of application delivery. AppSync is GraphQL APIs; Batch runs batch jobs; DataSync moves data.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service or functionality is by default highly available?
questions.explanation: Amazon Aurora's storage is replicated six ways across three AZs by default — highly available out of the box. RDS Multi-AZ requires opt-in; EC2 and NAT instances are single by default.

questions.prompt: A business requires data migration from its on-premises environment to the AWS Cloud. The business demands extremely elastic and efficient connection. Which AWS service satisfies these criteria?
questions.explanation: AWS Storage Gateway provides hybrid storage with elastic and efficient connectivity between on-premises and AWS storage. Glacier is archival, AWS Backup orchestrates managed backups, EFS is shared file storage in AWS.

questions.prompt: A company wants to perform sentiment analysis on customer service email messages that it receives. The company wants to identify whether the customer service engagement was positive or negative. Which AWS service should the company use to perform this analysis?
questions.explanation: Amazon Comprehend is the managed NLP service that performs sentiment analysis on text. Textract extracts text from documents, Translate translates languages, Rekognition analyzes images/video.

questions.prompt: What is the total amount of storage offered by Amazon S3?
questions.explanation: Amazon S3 offers virtually unlimited storage capacity (5 TB is the maximum object size, not the total). Buckets can hold an unlimited number of objects.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service or functionality is exclusively accessible to AWS Enterprise Support customers?
questions.explanation: The AWS Concierge Support team is exclusively available to Enterprise Support customers. Trusted Advisor and Support cases are available on multiple tiers; Connect is unrelated.

questions.prompt: Which resource is the MOST EFFECTIVE for keeping current on AWS security announcements?
questions.explanation: AWS Security Bulletins are the official channel for AWS to publish security advisories and announcements. Personal Health Dashboard is about service health; Secrets Manager stores secrets; Inspector scans for vulnerabilities.

questions.prompt: Which AWS services aid in application performance optimization by lowering latency associated with global content access? (Select two.)
questions.explanation: Amazon CloudFront (CDN at edge) and AWS Global Accelerator (anycast at edge) both lower global latency. VPN and Direct Connect are for specific endpoints; Glacier is archival storage.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service should a company use to create a NoSQL database?
questions.explanation: Amazon DynamoDB is AWS's managed NoSQL database (key-value and document). Aurora and RDS are relational; Redshift is a data warehouse; Neptune is a graph DB.

questions.prompt: Which techniques are available for deriving AWS charges by department? (Select two.)

options.text: Enable multi-factor authentication for the AWS account root user.
Create separate accounts for each department.
Use tags to associate each instance with a particular department.

questions.explanation: Separate accounts for each department (under one organization for billing) and cost allocation tags on resources are the two standard ways to break out AWS spend by department.

questions.prompt: Which AWS services can be utilized to automate infrastructure? (Select two.)
questions.explanation: AWS CloudFormation (IaC templates) and AWS OpsWorks (Chef/Puppet-based config management) both automate infrastructure. CloudFront is CDN; Batch runs jobs; QuickSight is BI.

questions.prompt: The solutions architect of a business wants to deploy a few Amazon EC2 instances in an already-existing AWS account. Before the firm can accept the request, it wants a cost estimate. Which AWS tool should be used to generate the cost estimate?
questions.explanation: AWS Pricing Calculator generates cost estimates for proposed deployments before resources are launched. Cost Explorer/CUR/Budgets work on actual spend.

questions.prompt: A company is migrating to Amazon S3. The company needs to transfer 60 TB of data from an on-premises data center to AWS within 10 days. Which AWS service should the company use to accomplish this migration?
questions.explanation: 60 TB in 10 days from on-prem to S3 calls for AWS Snowball — physical appliances ship in days. DMS migrates databases, Direct Connect is a network link (still slow at that volume), Glacier is archival storage.

questions.prompt: What type of database is Amazon DynamoDB?
questions.explanation: Amazon DynamoDB is a key-value (and document) NoSQL database.

questions.prompt: A retail company has recently migrated its website to AWS. The company wants to ensure that it is protected from SQL injection attacks. Which AWS service or feature can be used to create a custom rule that blocks SQL injection attacks?
questions.explanation: AWS WAF lets you create custom rules (and use managed rule groups) to block SQL injection and other application-layer attacks. Security groups, NACLs, and Shield operate at L3/L4, not L7.

questions.prompt: Which AWS services enable users to link their AWS Cloud resources to their on-premises resources? (Select two.)
questions.explanation: AWS VPN (Site-to-Site VPN over internet) and AWS Direct Connect (private dedicated circuit) connect AWS Cloud resources to on-premises networks.

questions.prompt: While an Amazon EC2 instance is only activated when it is required, it must stay active throughout the life of the procedure. What is the most acceptable method of purchase?
questions.explanation: On-Demand Instances activate when needed, stay active as long as required, and bill per second — perfect when usage is intermittent but the instance must remain on for a procedure. Spot can be interrupted; RIs/Dedicated are commitment-based.

questions.prompt: A business's operations are conducted on-site. The business wants to anticipate the costs associated with hosting a major application on AWS. Which AWS service or tool does the business have access to in order to collect this information?
questions.explanation: AWS Pricing Calculator estimates AWS costs for a proposed hosting plan before the workload is migrated. Budgets/Cost Explorer/Trusted Advisor analyze existing usage.

questions.prompt: How can a client strengthen the security of their Amazon Web Services (AWS) account logins? (Select two.)

options.text: Use Amazon Cognito to manage access

questions.explanation: Enabling MFA and configuring a strong IAM password policy directly strengthen account login security. ACM is for TLS certificates; Cognito is for app users; Organizations is multi-account governance.

questions.prompt: A big firm often has a single Amazon Web Services (AWS) account. What are the benefits of repurposing a single AWS account to create additional AWS accounts? (Select two.)

options.text: It allows for administrative isolation between different workloads.
Discounts can be applied on a quarterly basis by submitting cases in the AWS Management Console.
Transitioning objects from Amazon S3 to Amazon S3 Glacier in separate AWS accounts will be less expensive.
Having multiple accounts reduces the risks associated with malicious activity targeted at a single account.
Amazon QuickSight offers access to a cost tool that provides application-specific recommendations for environments running in multiple accounts.

questions.explanation: Splitting one account into many AWS accounts provides administrative isolation between workloads (blast radius) and reduces the risk that compromise of one account exposes everything else.

questions.prompt: Which solution enables a business with numerous AWS accounts to consolidate its consumption in order to qualify for bulk discounts?
questions.explanation: AWS Organizations consolidates usage across member accounts so the org qualifies for tiered volume discounts that no single account could reach alone.

questions.prompt: Which AWS services may be protected against typical online exploitations using AWS WAF? (Select two.)
questions.explanation: AWS WAF protects Amazon CloudFront, Amazon API Gateway, Application Load Balancers, and AWS AppSync. Of the listed options, CloudFront and API Gateway qualify. Route 53, Transfer Family, and Site-to-Site VPN are not WAF-protected.

questions.prompt: A business uses an Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling policy in conjunction with an Application Load Balancer to restore sick apps running on Amazon EC2 instances automatically. This activity pertains to which pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework?
questions.explanation: Automatically recovering unhealthy applications via ASG + ALB is the Reliability pillar applied in practice — recovering from failure without manual intervention.

questions.prompt: A business wants to migrate current software licenses to AWS, however the licensing strategy involves licensing physical cores. How can the organization do this in the AWS Cloud?

options.text: Launch an Amazon EC2 instance with default tenancy.
Launch an Amazon EC2 instance on a Dedicated Host.

questions.explanation: Dedicated Hosts let you Bring Your Own License (BYOL) for software licensed by physical cores/sockets/VMs. Default tenancy, On-Demand Capacity Reservations, and Dedicated Reserved Instances don't expose the physical core count for BYOL purposes.

questions.prompt: Which service offers nearly infinite online storage for very durable objects?
questions.explanation: Amazon S3 offers virtually unlimited highly durable (11 9s) object storage. Redshift is a data warehouse, EFS is file, ECS is containers.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an advantage that users experience when they move on-premises workloads to the AWS Cloud?

options.text: Elimination of expenses for running and maintaining data centers
Price discounts that are identical to discounts from hardware providers
Distribution of all operational controls to AWS

questions.explanation: Moving on-prem workloads to AWS eliminates ongoing data-center run and maintenance expenses (power, cooling, real estate, hardware refresh). The other options overstate AWS's role.

questions.prompt: Which AWS services are available for file storage? (Select two.)
questions.explanation: Of the listed services, Amazon S3 (object storage commonly described as storing files) and AWS Storage Gateway (which includes a File Gateway for NFS/SMB access) are the most file-oriented choices. Lambda and SageMaker aren't storage; EBS is block storage.

questions.prompt: A media business wants to disseminate video material through the internet to millions of viewers globally. The organization wishes to use AWS's worldwide network backbone in order to deliver cached material with minimal latency and fast data transfer rates. Which AWS service will satisfy these criteria?
questions.explanation: Amazon CloudFront caches video content at the global edge network for low-latency streaming with high data transfer rates over the AWS backbone.

questions.prompt: Which AWS advantage allows customers to construct cloud infrastructure that spans various geographic areas and is linked through a low-latency, high-performance network?
questions.explanation: "Global reach" describes AWS's ability to deploy infrastructure across many geographic Regions interconnected by AWS's private low-latency backbone.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service or feature enables businesses to take advantage of AWS's volume discounts?
questions.explanation: AWS Organizations consolidates usage across accounts, qualifying the organization for AWS's tiered volume discounts. Savings Plans and Pricing Calculator are different mechanisms.

questions.prompt: Which AWS IAM feature is used to link numerous users with a set of permissions?
questions.explanation: IAM Groups link multiple users to a shared set of permissions — attach a policy to the group and every member inherits it.

questions.prompt: A social media firm has been tasked with the responsibility of launching a new function. Users will be able to share photographs that will be seen by other users worldwide with little delay. Which AWS service or feature should the business use in order to achieve this demand the most cost-effectively?
questions.explanation: Amazon CloudFront caches images at the global edge network for low-latency viewing worldwide and is the most cost-effective option for serving global media.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service enables application decoupling?
questions.explanation: Amazon SQS provides message queues that decouple producers from consumers — applications can scale and fail independently.

questions.prompt: Amazon EC2 web servers connect to a traditional application operating in a corporate data center. What phrase would be appropriate to use to describe this model?
questions.explanation: EC2 web servers in AWS calling out to a traditional on-premises application is a hybrid architecture by definition.

questions.prompt: Which design principle is included in the operational excellence pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework?
questions.explanation: "Anticipate failure" is a stated design principle of the Operational Excellence pillar. Documentation is supportive but not the named principle; performance and cost belong to other pillars.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is the customer responsible for updating and patching, according to the AWS shared responsibility model?

options.text: Amazon FSx for Windows File Server
AWS Directory Service for Microsoft Active Directory
Amazon RDS for Microsoft SQL Server

questions.explanation: Amazon WorkSpaces virtual Windows desktops are customer-managed at the OS level — the customer is responsible for keeping the desktop OS updated and patched. FSx, Directory Service, and RDS are managed by AWS at the OS level.

questions.prompt: A previously utilized Amazon EC2 instance for development is no longer available and does not display in the AWS Management Console. Which AWS service should be utilized to discover what activity resulted in the inaccessibility of this EC2 instance?
questions.explanation: AWS CloudTrail records the API call that terminated the EC2 instance — including the caller, time, and source IP — so you can investigate the unavailability. CloudWatch Logs may have related logs but doesn't directly identify the API action.

questions.prompt: A Cloud Practitioner is required to retain data for seven years in order to comply with regulatory standards. Which AWS service meets this need for the LEAST amount of money?
questions.explanation: Amazon S3 Glacier is the lowest-cost option for long-term retention (7 years). S3 Standard is far pricier; Snowball is transfer; Redshift is analytics.

questions.prompt: Why is an AWS Well-Architected review a critical part of the cloud design process?

options.text: A Well-Architected review is mandatory before a workload can run on AWS.
A Well-Architected review helps identify design gaps and helps evaluate design decisions and related documents.
A Well-Architected review is an audit mechanism that is a part of requirements for service level agreements.
A Well-Architected review eliminates the need for ongoing auditing and compliance tests.

questions.explanation: A Well-Architected review helps identify design gaps and evaluate design decisions against best practices. It is not mandatory, not part of SLAs, and does not eliminate ongoing audits.

questions.prompt: A company implements an Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling policy along with an Application Load Balancer to automatically recover unhealthy applications that run on Amazon EC2 instances. Which pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework does this action cover?
questions.explanation: Auto-recovering unhealthy instances with ASG + ALB is a Reliability pillar pattern (recover from failure).

questions.prompt: What does AWS's obligation under the shared responsibility paradigm entail?

options.text: Updating the network ACLs to block traffic to vulnerable ports.
Patching operating systems running on Amazon EC2 instances.
Updating the firmware on the underlying EC2 hosts.
Updating the security group rules to block traffic to the vulnerable ports.

questions.explanation: Updating firmware on the underlying EC2 host hardware is AWS's responsibility. NACL/security group rules and EC2 OS patching are customer responsibilities.

questions.prompt: AWS CloudFormation is intended to assist the user in the following ways:
questions.explanation: AWS CloudFormation models and provisions AWS resources from declarative templates — that's its core purpose. It doesn't update application code, set up data lakes, or produce billing reports.

questions.prompt: Which AWS Cloud benefit is shown by an architecture's ability to withstand failures with minimal downtime?
questions.explanation: Withstanding failures with minimal downtime is the definition of high availability. Agility is deployment speed, elasticity is matching capacity to demand, scalability is growth without redesign.

questions.prompt: Under the AWS shared responsibility model, which task is the customer's responsibility when managing AWS Lambda functions?

options.text: Scaling Lambda resources according to demand

questions.explanation: With AWS Lambda the customer creates and versions function code. AWS handles servers/OS, scaling, and runtime updates.

questions.prompt: A company needs to generate reports that can break down cloud costs by product, by company-defined tags, and by hour, day, and month. Which AWS tool should the company use to meet these requirements?

options.text: Reserved Instance utilization and coverage reports

questions.explanation: The AWS Cost and Usage Report provides the most granular spend data — by product, tag, account, hour/day/month. RI and SP utilization reports cover purchasing, Budgets reports show threshold tracking.

questions.prompt: A business wishes to grant access to Amazon RDS to one of its workers. Additionally, the corporation wishes to confine contact to the AWS CLI and AWS software development kits (SDKs). Which combination of measures should the business do in order to satisfy these needs while adhering to the concept of least privilege? (Select two.)

options.text: Create an IAM user and provide AWS Management Console access only.
Create an IAM user and provide programmatic access only.
Create an IAM role and provide AWS Management Console access only.
Create an IAM policy with administrator access and attach it to the IAM user.
Create an IAM policy with Amazon RDS access and attach it to the IAM user.

questions.explanation: Create an IAM user with programmatic access only (no console password) and attach an IAM policy granting only Amazon RDS access. This combines least privilege with the CLI/SDK-only requirement.

questions.prompt: Which of the following guidelines constitutes a well-architected design philosophy for cloud application development?

options.text: Keep static data closer to compute resources.
Design for automated recovery from failure.

questions.explanation: Designing for automated recovery from failure is a core Well-Architected design philosophy. Provisioning for peak, tight coupling, and ignoring data locality are anti-patterns.

questions.prompt: A company has a serverless application that includes an Amazon API Gateway function, and an Amazon DynamoDB database. Which AWS service can the company use to trace user requests as they pass through components?
questions.explanation: AWS X-Ray traces user requests as they travel through distributed (including serverless) application components, surfacing latency and errors. CloudTrail is API audit, CloudWatch is metrics/logs, Inspector scans for vulnerabilities.

questions.prompt: A company needs to set up a petabyte-scale data warehouse in the AWS Cloud. Which AWS service will meet this requirement?
questions.explanation: Amazon Redshift is the petabyte-scale managed data warehouse on AWS. DynamoDB is NoSQL OLTP; RDS is relational OLTP; ElastiCache is in-memory.

questions.prompt: What are the immediate advantages of AWS Cloud computing? (Select two.)

options.text: Capital expenses are replaced with variable expenses.
AWS holds responsibility for security in the cloud.

questions.explanation: Replacing capital expenses with variable expenses, and increased business agility, are immediate AWS Cloud benefits. AWS reduces IT staff burden, customers don't control physical infra, and security is shared (not solely AWS).

questions.prompt: The following are the economical advantages of using AWS: (Choose two.)
questions.explanation: AWS reduces both Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) and operating expenditure (opex) for typical workloads. AWS does not increase capex; deferred payment plans and credit lines aren't AWS economic advantages.

questions.prompt: Which AWS service is always provided at no charge?
questions.explanation: AWS IAM is an "always free" service — there is no charge for IAM itself. S3, ELB, and WAF all have usage-based charges.

questions.prompt: Which acts exemplify excellent practices for AWS IAM use? (Select two.)

options.text: Share the security credentials among users of AWS accounts who are in the same Region
Use access keys to log in to the AWS Management Console
Rotate access keys on a regular basis
Avoid using IAM roles to delegate permissions

questions.explanation: Configuring a strong password policy and rotating access keys regularly are stated IAM best practices. Sharing credentials, using access keys for console login, and avoiding roles are all anti-patterns.

questions.prompt: A business wants to establish templates that it may reuse when deploying numerous AWS resources. Which Amazon Web Services (AWS) offering or functionality can the business employ to achieve this requirement?
questions.explanation: AWS CloudFormation lets a business build reusable templates that deploy AWS resources in a controlled, repeatable way. Marketplace sells software, AMI is one instance image, OpsWorks is Chef/Puppet config management.

questions.prompt: How can a business use AWS to lower its Total Cost of Ownership (TCO)?

options.text: By having no responsibility for third-party license costs

questions.explanation: AWS lowers TCO by minimizing large up-front capital expenditures (no buying servers/network gear); customers still have operational expenses and third-party license costs.

questions.prompt: A business's website is hosted on Amazon EC2 instances. The firm must guarantee that the website is accessible to a worldwide audience and has a low latency for visitors. Which Amazon Web Services (AWS) offering should the business employ to achieve these requirements?
questions.explanation: Amazon CloudFront's global edge network caches content close to viewers, guaranteeing low latency for global users of an EC2-hosted website. Route 53 is DNS; ELB is regional; Lambda is compute.
